name: Company Census File
description: The Company Census File contains records for active, inactive, and pending entities registered with FMCSA. Entities include those entities subject to the FMCSR, HMR, or intrastate non-Hazardous Material (HM) carriers. To identify each entity, FMCSA assigns a unique number to each entity record. This number is referred to as the USDOT number. Each Census record contains entity identifying data, business operations data, equipment and driver data, and carrier review data.

name: Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) Vehicle Trajectories and Supporting Data
provider: U.S. Department of Transportation Intelligent Transportation Systems Joint Program Office (JPO) -- Recommended citation: "U.S. Department of Transportation Federal Highway Administration. (2016). Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) Vehicle Trajectories and Supporting Data. [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1504477"

columns.name: v_Class
columns.description: Vehicle type: 1 - motorcycle, 2 - auto, 3 - truck

columns.name: Local_X
columns.description:
Lateral (X) coordinate of the front center of the vehicle in feet with respect to
the left-most edge of the section in the direction of travel.

columns.name: Movement
columns.description:
Movement of the vehicle. 1 - through (TH), 2 - left-turn (LT), 3 - right-turn
(RT).

columns.name: Int_ID
columns.description:
Intersection in which the vehicle is traveling. Intersections are numbered
from 1 to 4, with intersection 1 at the southernmost, and intersection 4 at the northernmost
section of the study area. Value of “0” means that the vehicle was not in the immediate vicinity of
an intersection and that the vehicle instead identifies with a section of Lankershim Boulevard
(Section_ID, below). Please refer to the data analysis report for more detailed information.

columns.name: Section_ID
columns.description:
Section in which the vehicle is traveling. Lankershim Blvd is divided into five
sections (south of intersection 1; between intersections 1 and 2, 2 and 3, 3 and 4; and north of
intersection 4). Value of “0” means that the vehicle does not identify with a section of Lankershim
Boulevard and that the vehicle was in the immediate vicinity of an intersection (Int_ID above).
Please refer to the data analysis report for more detailed information

columns.name: v_Acc
columns.description: Instantaneous acceleration of vehicle in feet/second square.

columns.name: v_Vel
columns.description: Instantaneous velocity of vehicle in feet/second.

columns.name: Direction
columns.description:
Moving direction of the vehicle. 1 - east-bound (EB), 2 - north-bound (NB), 3 -
west-bound (WB), 4 - south-bound (SB).

columns.name: Total_Frames
columns.description: Total number of frames in which the vehicle appears in this data set

columns.name: v_Width
columns.description: Width of vehicle in feet

columns.name: v_length
columns.description: Length of vehicle in feet

columns.name: Preceding
columns.description:
Vehicle ID of the lead vehicle in the same lane. A value of '0' represents no
preceding vehicle - occurs at the end of the study section and off-ramp due to the fact that only
complete trajectories were recorded by this data collection effort (vehicles already in the section
at the start of the study period were not recorded).

columns.name: Following
columns.description:
Vehicle ID of the vehicle following the subject vehicle in the same lane. A
value of '0' represents no following vehicle - occurs at the beginning of the study section and onramp
due to the fact that only complete trajectories were recorded by this data collection effort
(vehicle that did not traverse the downstream boundaries of the section by the end of the study
period were not recorded).

columns.name: Global_Time
columns.description: Elapsed time in milliseconds since Jan 1, 1970.

columns.name: Global_Y
columns.description:
Y Coordinate of the front center of the vehicle in feet based on CA State
Plane III in NAD83.

columns.name: Global_X
columns.description:
X Coordinate of the front center of the vehicle in feet based on CA State
Plane III in NAD83.
Attribute Domain Val

columns.name: Space_Headway
columns.description:
Space Headway in feet. Spacing provides the distance between the frontcenter
of a vehicle to the front-center of the preceding vehicle.

columns.name: Time_Headway
columns.description:
Time Headway in seconds. Time Headway provides the time to travel from
the front-center of a vehicle (at the speed of the vehicle) to the front-center of the preceding
vehicle. A headway value of 99

columns.name: Vehicle_ID
columns.description: Vehicle identification number (ascending by time of entry into section). REPEATS ARE NOT ASSOCIATED.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Name of street or freeway

columns.name: Local_Y
columns.description:
Longitudinal (Y) coordinate of the front center of the vehicle in feet with
respect to the entry edge of the section in the direction of travel.

columns.name: D_Zone
columns.description:
Destination zones of the vehicles, i.e., the place where the vehicles exit the
tracking system. There are 10 destinations in the study area, numbered from 201 through 211.
Origin 102 is a one-way off-ramp; hence there is no associated destination number 202. Please
refer to the data analysis report for more detailed information.

columns.name: Frame_ID
columns.description: Frame Identification number (ascending by start time)

columns.name: Lane_ID
columns.description:
Current lane position of vehicle. Lane 1 is farthest left lane; lane 5 is farthest
right lane. Lane 6 is the auxiliary lane between Ventura Boulevard on-ramp and the Cahuenga
Boulevard off-ramp. Lane 7 is the on-ramp at Ventura Boulevard, and Lane 8 is the off-ramp at
Cahuenga Boulevard.

columns.name: O_Zone
columns.description:
Origin zones of the vehicles, i.e., the place where the vehicles enter the
tracking system. There are 11 origins in the study area, numbered from 101 through 111. Please
refer to the data analysis report for more detailed information.

description: Click “Export” on the right to download the vehicle trajectory data. The associated metadata and additional data can be downloaded below under "Attachments". Researchers for the Next Generation Simulation (NGSIM) program collected detailed vehicle trajectory data on southbound US 101 and Lankershim Boulevard in Los Angeles, CA, eastbound I-80 in Emeryville, CA and Peachtree Street in Atlanta, Georgia. name: Recalls Data

columns.name: Recall Description
columns.description: Displays a summary of the problem that prompted the recall. The description includes the product make, model, and model year.

columns.name: Recall Type
columns.description: Displays the recall category. If the recall involves a noncompliant product, the word "compliance" appears in this field. If the recall involves a defective product, the word "Defect" appears. If the recall involves a product that requires a safety improvement, the phrase "safety improvement" appears.

columns.name: Component
columns.description: Displays the name of the defective/noncompliant component associated with the product.

columns.name: Potentially Affected
columns.description: Displays the number of products that are potentially affected by the recall.

columns.name: Park Outside Advisory
columns.description: Advisory for owners to park outside and away from structures because vehicles can catch fire.

columns.name: Do Not Drive Advisory
columns.description: Advisory for owners of these vehicles should park them immediately until the recall remedy is completed.

columns.name: Corrective Action
columns.description: Displays a brief description of the proposed solution to the recall.

columns.name: Report Received Date
columns.description: Displays the date that NHTSA received the Defect and Noncompliance report from the manufacturer.

columns.name: Completion Rate % (Blank - Not Reported)
columns.description: Displays a percentage that indicates how many units of the recalled population have been remedied. Blank value indicates it has not been reported

columns.name: NHTSA ID
columns.description: NHTSA CAMPAIGN NUMBER, Displays the recall number. The recall number is composed of the two-digit year, the one-character recall type, the two-character recall sub-type, the three-character recall number for the year, and the three-character recall subject.

columns.name: Subject
columns.description: Displays the name of the product and the defect that is the subject of the recall.

columns.name: Consequence Summary
columns.description: Displays a summary of the consequences of the defect or noncompliance.

columns.name: Mfr Campaign Number
columns.description: Displays the identification number assigned to the recall by the manufacturer.

columns.name: Manufacturer
columns.description: Displays the name of the product manufacturer.

columns.name: Recall Link
columns.description: Displays a link for Information on the recall on the NHTSA web site.

description: About the Data: The dataset includes recall information related to specific NHTSA campaigns. Users can filter based on characteristics like manufacturer and component. The dataset can also be filtered by recall type: tires, vehicles, car seats, and equipment. The earliest campaign data is from 1966. The dataset displays the completion rate from the latest Recall Quarterly Report or Annual Report data from Year 2015 Quarter 1 (2015-1) onward. columns.description: Total number of people injured in the accident, as reported on the Form 55a. The JOINTCD=1 record contains the total number and other reports contain zero. This is the official injury count per accident, not per report. There is only one report for each accident containing the sum for all reports for one unique accident. description: A highway-rail crossing is a location where a public highway, road, street, or private roadway, including associated sidewalks and pathways, crosses one or more railroad tracks. All such crossings are reported by railroads and state Department of Transportations to the FRA on Form FRA F 6180.71 U.S. DOT Crossing Inventory Form. Each location has an assigned U.S. DOT Grade Crossing Identification Number, or crossing ID. This dataset is in a user-friendly human-readable format. It contains the cur

name: Work Zone Data Feed Registry
provider: United States Department of Transportation (USDOT) Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Joint Program Office (JPO) --- Recommended citation: "U.S. Department of Transportation Intelligent Transportation Systems Joint Program Office. (2020). Work Zone Data Exchange (WZDx) Feed Registry. [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1518702"

columns.name: version
columns.description: The WZDx or CWZ version number that the feed follows between the start and end date of the entry. WZDx versions are reported by their version number in semantic versioning (1.1, 2.0, 3.0, 3.1, 4.0, 4.1, and 4.2). CWZ version 1.0 feed is recorded as “CWZ 1.0”

columns.name: url
columns.description: URL to the API feed.

columns.name: active
columns.description: Whether or not the feed is currently active.

columns.name: issuingOrganization
columns.description: Organization issuing and maintaining the work zone data exchange API feed.

columns.name: StateCoordinate
columns.description: Coordinate of state column.

columns.name: apiKeyURL
columns.description: The URL for the sign-up page to create an API Key for this data feed

columns.name: feedName
columns.description: Identifier name of the feed as designated by ITS DataHub when the feed is added to the registry, usually named based on the orginzation providing data.

columns.name: format
columns.description: Format of the data in the API feed. Possible values: xml, json, geojson

columns.name: DatafeedUpdateFrequency
columns.description: The frequency at which the data feed is updated and made available through the data feed. Format shall include value + units such as 30s, 15m, or 24h where: s = seconds m = minutes h-hours.

columns.name: sdate
columns.description: Start date at which the metadata of the entry (e.g. version number, update frequency, etc) hold true.

columns.name: edate
columns.description: End date at which the metadata of the entry (e.g. version number, update frequency, etc) hold true. If no value is given, the metadata in the entry currently hold true.

columns.name: needAPIKey
columns.description: True if the feed requires the user to create a unique API Key first. API Key URL will direct you to sign up page

columns.name: state
columns.description: Lowercase name of the US state where the WZDx feed is from.

columns.name: United States
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'United States' (8fe2-rd7y) the point in column 'geocoded_column' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

description: This dataset contains the up-to-date metadata on Work Zone feeds that meet the Work Zone Data Exchange (WZDx) specification or Connected Work Zone (CWZ) Standard and are registered with USDOT ITS DataHub. The current work zone data from each feed can be accessed through their respective API links. To access the Work Zone Data Feed Registry’s list of current work zone data feeds, click the “Data” tab to view current data or the “Export” button to download as a CSV or access the API endpoint. Some

name: Vehicle Inspection File
description: Federal and State field enforcement staff performs Inspections on Interstate and Intrastate Motor Carriers and Hazardous Materials carriers. Violations of the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations (FMCSRs) severe enough may result in a vehicle and/or driver being placed "out-of-service." The data collected from inspection activity is collected and stored in the FMCSA Motor Carrier Management Information System (MCMIS) Inspection Data Files. Due to privacy restrictions driver information is no

name: Enhancing Microsimulation Models for Improved Work Zone Planning: Car-Following Data from Western Massachusetts (Instances)

columns.name: rural_urban
columns.description: Whether the road is urban or rural. Valid values are: U: Urban. Instances within 2 miles of an on-ramp or off-ramp to Springfield, MA are considered urban. R: Rural. Other instances are considered rural.

columns.name: leader_radar
columns.description: Records the sensor that was used to measure information about the lead vehicle. Valid values are: 0: The lead vehicle was detected by the front radar. 1: The lead vehicle was detected by the rear radar. 10: The IRV is the lead vehicle.

columns.name: follower_veh_type
columns.description: A description of the type of the following vehicle. Valid values are: PC: passenger car. HV: heavy vehicle. B: bus. M: motorcycle.

columns.name: end_time
columns.description: The end time of the car-following instance, measured since the start of the data collection run, in HH:MM:SS.sss format.

columns.name: road_type
columns.description: A description of the road the vehicle is traveling on. Valid values are: HW: Highway. A U.S. or State route, which is not an interstate.  IS: Interstate. A numbered Interstate highway with two or more lanes. In this dataset, this is I-91. AW: Advanced Warning. The vehicle is traveling on the freeway towards the work zone.  The vehicle has already passed advanced warning signage for the work zone (ex. "Work Zone in 1 mile") TZ: Taper Zone. The vehicle is entering and/or traversing the freeway work zone's taper zone WZ1: Work Zone with Lane Closure. The vehicle is traversing the buffer zone and/or the work zone, for a freeway work zone with lane closure. WZ2: Work Zone without Lane Closure. The vehicle is traversing the buffer zone and/or the work zone, for a freeway work zone without lane closure. Because some work zones traversed along this route were collected on arterials as well as freeways, “HW” or “IS” are appended to the end of codes AW, TZ, WZ1, WZ2 to indicate the type of road being worked on.

columns.name: run_instance_id
columns.description: An ID was assigned by the data processing team to each car-following instance recorded during each driver’s run. Note that this count resets for each driver.

columns.name: num_lanes
columns.description: Number of travel lanes in the direction of travel.

columns.name: parent_instance_id
columns.description: During some car-following instances, the road characteristics (road_type, congestion, or speed_limit) changed while the same leader/follower pair continued to follow each other. These instances are represented by multiple records in the table, which have the same parent_instance_id but different run_instance_id values.

columns.name: congestion
columns.description: Records the traffic conditions. Valid values are: C: Congested. Because of surrounding traffic, the travel speed of the subject vehicle is below 35 mph (or 15.6 m/s) in a non-work zone, or below 25 mph (or 11.1 m/s) in a work zone. U: Uncongested. Traffic conditions allow the subject vehicle to travel 35 mph (or 15.6 m/s) or faster in a non-work zone, or 25 mph (or 11.1 m/s) or faster in a work zone.

columns.name: speed_limit
columns.description: The speed limit of the section of route on which the driver is traveling, in miles per hour.

columns.name: wz_status
columns.description: Indicates if the work zone is passive or active. Valid values include: P: no workers present; A: active work zone, workers present.

columns.name: wz_markers
columns.description: This field indicates the type of physical delineators used in the work zone. Valid values include: C: Cones. D: Drums or Barrels. JB: Jersey Barriers. Multiple values can be present in this field (comma-separated).

columns.name: start_time
columns.description: The start time of the car-following instance, measured since the start of the data collection run, in HH:MM:SS.sss format.

columns.name: follower_radar
columns.description: Records the sensor that was used to measure information about the following vehicle. Valid values are: 0: The following vehicle was detected by the front radar. 1: The following vehicle was detected by the rear radar. 10: The IRV is the following vehicle.

columns.name: signage
columns.description: Temporary signage in the advanced warning zone, or message board signage in the taper zone. Valid values include: W20-1, W20-3, W4-2, W20-4, W8-1: see MUTCD sign designations for description. FA: flashing arrow on message board. Multiple values can be present in this field (comma-separated).

columns.name: run_id
columns.description: ID of the data collection run (foreign key to runs table)

columns.name: global_instance_id
columns.description: Unique identifier of each instance record (primary key). Defined by (run_id*1000 + run_instance_id)

columns.name: leader_veh_type
columns.description: A description of the type of the lead vehicle. Valid values are: PC: passenger car. HV: heavy vehicle. B: bus. M: motorcycle.

columns.name: wz_enforcement
columns.description: Whether law enforcement vehicles or officers were present at the time of data capture. Valid values include: Y: yes; N: no.

columns.name: current_lane
columns.description: Current travel lane, numbered from 1 (leftmost lane) to num_lanes (rightmost lane).

The USDOT Volpe National Transportation Systems Center (Volpe Center) identified, isolated, and classified individual car following instances f

name: Highway Data Element Dictionary

columns.name: Precision Number
columns.description: The number of decimal places permitted in any associated data element values. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Precision+Number

columns.name: Data Asset Abbreviation
columns.description: The abbreviation for the data asset or data set. A data asset is collection of data elements or datasets that make sense to group together. A given Data Asset may represent an entire database consisting of multiple distinct entity classes, or may represent a single entity class. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Data+Asset+Abbreviation

columns.name: Subject Category Code
columns.description: This is the primary high-level taxonomy subject category for the data element. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Subject+Category+Code

columns.name: Data Asset Name
columns.description: The name of the data asset or data set.   A data asset is collection of data elements or datasets that make sense to group together. A given Data Asset may represent an entire database consisting of multiple distinct entity classes, or may represent a single entity class. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Data+Asset+Name

columns.name: Pattern Text
columns.description: Pattern is a constraint on the value space of a datatype which is achieved by constraining the lexical space to literals which match a specific pattern. The value of pattern should be a regular expression. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Pattern+Number

columns.name: Description Text
columns.description: The definition text is a statement hich specifies the meaning of the Data Element. It may additionally record a source for the text. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Description+Text

columns.name: Unit of Measure
columns.description: Magnitude for some quantity, either physical or immaterial. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Unit+of+Measure

columns.name: Data Type Code
columns.description: The format used for the collection of letters, digits, and/or symbols, to depict values of a data element, determined by the operations that may be performed on a data element. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Data+Type+Code

columns.name: Data Element Name
columns.description: Unit of data that is considered in context to be indivisible. A Data Element is considered to be a basic unit of data of interest to an organization. It is a unit of data for which the definition, identification, representation, and permissible values are specified by means of a set of attributes. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Data+Element+Name

columns.name: System Number
columns.description: This is the number of systems the data element is used.  The current systems that have been crosswalked are FMIS, HPMS, TMAS, and LTPP.

columns.name: Business Owner Name
columns.description: The organization and contact within the organization that is responsible for the definition and other mandatory attributes by which the metadata item is specified. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Business+Owner+Name

columns.name: Maximum Length
columns.description: This field is the maximum number of units of length, where units of length varies depending on the type that is being derived from. The value of maxLength must be a nonNegativeInteger. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Maximum+Length

columns.name: Value Domain
columns.description: Value Domain is a class each instance of which models a value domain, a collection of permissible values. A value domain provides representation, but has no implication as to what data element concept the values are associated with, nor what the values mean. Permissible values are designations, bindings of signs (values) to their corresponding value meanings. https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Value+Domain

columns.name: Preferred Physical Name
columns.description: This is the preferred name for physical models or data definition language (DDL). https://data.transportation.gov/resource/3enx-g69f.csv?data_element_name=Preferred+Physical+Name

columns.description: Date data collection run occurred, in YYYY-MM-DD format.

columns.name: direction
columns.description: The direction of travel during the run. Valid values are: NB: northbound. SB southbound.

name: Texas DOT (TxDOT) Work Zone Data Schema Version 2.0
provider: U.S. Department of Transportation (USDOT) Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Joint Program Office (JPO) --- Recommended citation: "Texas Department of Transportation. (2020). Texas DOT (TxDOT) Work Zone Data Sample. [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1518704"

columns.name: geometry_linestring
columns.description: A coordinate pair or an array of coordinates. In either case, the first coordinate is the beginning point and the last coordinate is the ending point of the road event.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Short free text description of road event.

columns.name: creation_date
columns.description: The UTC time and date when the activity or event was created.

columns.name: update_date
columns.description: The UTC time and date when the activity or event was updated.

columns.name: types_of_work
columns.description: Stringified array of JSON objects indicating the type of work being done in a road event, if applicable (e.g. typical work zones), as well as noting if the type of work will result in an architectural change to the roadway. Each JSON object can contain the following fields: “type_of_work_id” - ID identifying the types_of_work record; “type_name” - a high-level text description of the type of work being done; “is_architectural_change” - an optional boolean flag indicating whether the type of work will result in an architectural change to the roadway.

columns.name: lanes
columns.description: Stringified array of JSON objects identifying and describing individual lanes within a road event. Each lane JSON object can contain the following fields: “lane_edge_reference” - the roadside edge from which lanes are assigned numbers. This field is required when “lane_number” is provided; “lane_number” - the number assigned to a lane (not including shoulders); “lane_status” - status of the lane for the traveling public; “lane_type” - an indication of the type of lane or shoulder; “lane_restrictions” - an array of lane_restriction JSON objects. Each lane_restriction JSON object contain the following fields: “restriction_type” - the type of restriction being enforced; “restriction_value” - the measure of the restriction type; “restriction_units” - units of measure for the restriction value.

columns.name: geometry_multipoint
columns.description: A coordinate pair or an array of coordinates. In either case, the first coordinate is the beginning point and the last coordinate is the ending point of the road event.

columns.name: beginning_milepost
columns.description: The linear distance measured against a milepost marker along a roadway where the event begins.

columns.name: beginning_cross_street
columns.description: Name or number of the nearest cross street along the roadway where the event begins.

columns.name: ending_cross_street
columns.description: Name or number of the nearest cross street along the roadway where the event ends.

columns.name: ending_milepost
columns.description: The linear distance measured against a milepost marker along a roadway where the event ends.

columns.name: start_date_accuracy
columns.description: A measure of how accurate the start Date Time is.

columns.name: total_num_lanes
columns.description: The total number of lanes associated with the road segment designated by the event geometry.

columns.name: end_date
columns.description: The UTC time and date when the event ends.

columns.name: vehicle_impact
columns.description: The impact to vehicular lanes along a single road in a single direction.

columns.name: road_event_id
columns.description: A unique identifier issued by the data feed provider to identify the work zone project or activity.

columns.name: workers_present
columns.description: A flag indicating that there are workers present in the event space.

columns.name: reduced_speed_limit
columns.description: The reduced speed limit posted within the event space.

columns.name: restrictions
columns.description: One or more road restrictions applying to the work zone road segment associated with the work zone delimited by semicolons.

columns.name: issuing_organization
columns.description: The organization issuing the data feed.

columns.name: direction
columns.description: The digitization direction of the road that is impacted by the event. This value is based on the standard naming for US roadways and indicates the direction the traffic flow regardless of the real heading angle.

columns.name: end_date_accuracy
columns.description: A measure of how accurate the end Date Time is.

columns.name: road_name
columns.description: Publicly known name of the road on which the event occurs.

columns.name: event_status
columns.description: The status of the event.

columns.name: ending_accuracy
columns.description: Indicates how the ending coordinate was defined.

columns.name: start_date
columns.description: The UTC time and date when the event begins.

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_feed_update_date
columns.description: The UTC date and time when the data feed was last updated.

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_version
columns.description: The specification version used to create the data feed.

columns.name: road_number
columns.description: The road number designated by a jurisdiction such as a county, state or interstate.

columns.name: subidentifier
columns.description: A unique identifier issued by data feed provider that provides additional references to project or activity.

columns.name: beginning_accuracy
columns.description: Indicates how the beginning coordinate was defined.

description: This dataset provides archived lane closure occurrences within the Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT) highway system in a tabular format. The ITS JPO has collections from 23 states including Texas covering various parts of the time period from 10/2019 to 08/2024 depending on when the feed was active. The data is split into two archive files, the raw data contains the collection of .json or .geojson files exactly as they were on the individual state’s WZDx feed at the time of collection.

name: Enhancing Microsimulation Models for Improved Work Zone Planning: Car-Following Data from Northern Virginia (Instances)

columns.name: follower_radar
columns.description: Records the sensor that was used to measure information about the following vehicle. Valid values are: 0: The following vehicle was detected by the front radar. 1: The following vehicle was detected by the rear radar. 10: The IRV is the following vehicle.

columns.name: end_time
columns.description: The end time of the car-following instance, measured since the start of the data collection run, in HH:MM:SS.sss format.

columns.name: start_time
columns.description: The start time of the car-following instance, measured since the start of the data collection run, in HH:MM:SS.sss format.

columns.name: road_type
columns.description: A description of the road the vehicle is traveling on. Valid values are: HW: Highway. A U.S. or State route, which is not an interstate. In this dataset, this only includes the George Washington Memorial Parkway. IS: Interstate. An Interstate highway with two or more lanes (no work zone present). In this dataset, these include I-395 and I-95. AW: Advanced Warning. The vehicle is traveling on the freeway towards the work zone.  The vehicle has already passed advanced warning signage for the work zone (ex. "Work Zone in 1 mile") TZ: Taper Zone. The vehicle is entering and/or traversing the freeway work zone's taper zone WZ1: Work Zone with Lane Closure. The vehicle is traversing the buffer zone and/or the work zone, for a freeway work zone with lane closure. WZ2: Work Zone without Lane Closure. The vehicle is traversing the buffer zone and/or the work zone, for a freeway work zone without lane closure. (Note: in this dataset, all work zones encountered were on Interstates).

columns.name: run_instance_id
columns.description: An ID was assigned by the data processing team to each car-following instance recorded during each driver’s run. Note that this count resets for each run.

columns.name: parent_instance_id
columns.description: During some car-following instances, the road characteristics (road_type, congestion, or speed_limit) changed while the same leader/follower pair continued to follow each other. These instances are represented by multiple records in the table, which have the same parent_instance_id but different run_instance_id values.

columns.name: leader_veh_type
columns.description: A description of the type of the lead vehicle. Valid values are: PC: passenger car. HV: heavy vehicle. B: bus. M: motorcycle.

columns.name: global_instance_id
columns.description: Unique identifier of each instance record (primary key). Defined by (run_id*1000 + run_instance_id)

columns.name: leader_radar
columns.description: Records the sensor that was used to measure information about the lead vehicle. Valid values are: 0: The lead vehicle was detected by the front radar. 1: The lead vehicle was detected by the rear radar. 10: The IRV is the lead vehicle.

columns.name: speed_limit
columns.description: The speed limit of the section of route on which the driver is traveling, in miles per hour.

columns.name: run_id
columns.description: ID assigned to the run/driver (foreign key to runs table)

columns.name: follower_veh_type
columns.description: A description of the type of the following vehicle. Valid values are: PC: passenger car. HV: heavy vehicle. B: bus. M: motorcycle.

columns.name: congestion
columns.description: Records the traffic conditions. Valid values are: C: Congested. Because of surrounding traffic, the travel speed of the subject vehicle is below 35 mph (or 15.6 m/s) in a non-work zone, or below 25 mph (or 11.1 m/s) in a work zone. U: Uncongested. Traffic conditions allow the subject vehicle to travel 35 mph (or 15.6 m/s) or faster in a non-work zone, or 25 mph (or 11.1 m/s) or faster in a work zone.

name: Takata Recall

columns.name: Net Air Bags Affected⁴
columns.description: Net Air Bags Affected = Total Air Bags Affected - Scrapped - Exported - Stolen - Other.

columns.name: Other³
columns.description: Other NHTSA has approved ACRO paragraph 45-48 submissions for the following Manufacturers: Starting 2/2/18 : General Motors, Honda, and Toyota: Starting 2/16/18 Mazda, Mitsubishi, Nissan.

columns.name: Manufacturer
columns.description: The name of the manufacturer

columns.name: Stolen
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles that have been stolen

columns.name: Vehicle Degradation
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles that are severely degraded such that they cannot be repaired without inordinate measures being taken, or performing the repair would introduce an unreasonable risk to safety to those performing the repair

columns.name: Net Air Bags Remaining⁵
columns.description: Net Air Bags Remaining = Net Air Bags Affected - Total Air Bags Repaired

columns.name: Affirmative Refusal
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles with owners who have affirmatively declined the repair. These individuals continue to receive outreach at a reduced cadence as compared to other owners.

columns.name: Exported
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles that have been exported

columns.name: Recall Campaign¹
columns.description:
Recall Campaigns Excluded (Not Launched as of 2/2/18):BMW-18V030, Daimler Vans-17V480 & 18V032, FCA-18V021, Ferarri-18V040, Ford-16V384, 17V024 & 18V046, GM-18V024 & 18V025, Jaguar LR-18V009, 18V010, 18V011 & 18V015, Mazda-16V499, 17V013, 18V016, 18V017 & 18V018, Mercedes Benz-16V363, 17V017 & 18V043, McLaren-18V007, Subaru-18V012, 18V013 & 18V014, Tesla-18V031, Toyota-18V024 & 18V025, Volkswagen-17V032 & 18V004.

Recall Campaigns Excluded (Motorcycles): Honda -16V347, 17V031 & 18V045.

columns.name: Total Air Bags Repaired
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall that have been replaced with a new inflator

columns.name: Completion Rate⁶
columns.description: Completion Rate = Total Air Bags Repaired / Net Air Bags Affected.

columns.name: Total Air Bags Affected
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall

columns.name: Report Date
columns.description: Record report date

columns.name: Priority Group²
columns.description: Priority Group reporting initiates on Report Date 8/18/17.

columns.name: Driver / Passenger
columns.description: Driver (DAB) / Passenger (PAB) side airbag indicator

columns.name: Aftermarket Modification
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles with aftermarket repairs or modifications that make a repair excessively difficult or impossible to complete

columns.name: Scrapped
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles that have been scrapped

columns.name: Nonresponsive
columns.description: Total number of inflators with an open Takata Recall in vehicles with owners who have been nonresponsive to recall notifications after significant outreach efforts. These individuals continue to receive outreach at a reduced cadence as compared to other owners.

description: Tens of millions of vehicles with Takata air bags are under recall. Long-term exposure to high heat and humidity can cause these air bags to explode when deployed. Such explosions have caused injuries and deaths. NHTSA urges vehicle owners to take a few simple steps to protect themselves and others from this very serious threat to safety. This dataset tracks various progress indicators for the recall.

description: This file contains the publicly available crash data used for the current month’s SMS calculations. Contains a record for each commercial motor vehicle involved in a crash including the U.S. DOT#, Report Number, Crash Date, State, and Vehicle Information. It also includes information about the severity of the crash (tow-away, injury, fatal) and the associated severity and time weights used in the SMS. File is comma delimited. One crash vehicle per row.

description: All impacts between railroad on-track equipment and highway users at a highway-rail grade crossing are reported by railroads to the FRA on Form FRA F 6180.57 Highway-Rail Grade Crossing Accident/Incident Report. This dataset is in a user-friendly human-readable format. To download the source dataset that contains raw data values, go here: https://data.transportation.gov/dataset/Form57-Source-Table/icqf-xf4w. The data dictionary can be found here: https://data.transportation.gov/api/views/7wn6-i5

name: Crash File
description: The FMCSA Crash File contains 59 data elements comprised of data received from state police crash reports involving drivers and vehicles of motor carriers operating in the U.S. Each report contains about 80 data elements pertaining to the motor carrier, driver, vehicles, and circumstances of a crash. Due to sensitive and/or privacy restrictions, driver data are not included in any crash files released to the public. The Crash File may contain multiple records for a crash. Separate reports are en

description: Casualties are reportable fatalities, injuries, or illnesses arising from the operation of a railroad. Casualties may be classified as either fatal or nonfatal. All casualty incidents are reported to the FRA on Form FRA F 6180.55a Injury/Illness Continuation Sheet. This dataset is in a user-friendly human-readable format. To download the source dataset that contains raw data values, go here: https://data.transportation.gov/dataset/Form55a-Source-Table/kuvg-3uwp. The data dictionary can be found

name: Inspections Per Unit
description: Identifies the type, make, company number, license plate, license plate state, VIN, CVSA Decal, and CVSA Number. There can be multiple Inspection Units per inspection.

name: Major Safety Events

columns.name: HazMat Type Description
columns.description: Text explanation from the 'Please describe" box used when "Other'' is selected for the "Haz Mat Type"

columns.name: Transit Worker Assault Flag
columns.description: When the value is True, the event is reported as an Assault on Transit Worker of any type. Otherwise, the value is false. Note, the value will appear as False for all events prior to 2023.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Rider Fatalities
columns.description: Count of vehicle passenger fatalities for the given event

columns.name: Fixed Route Flag
columns.description: Fixed Route Flag

columns.name: Pedestrian in Crosswalk Fatalities
columns.description: Count of Pedestrian: in crosswalk fatalities for the given event.

columns.name: Event Type Group
columns.description: A derived column to allow users to filter based on broader event type (e.g., Collision, Derailment, Assault).

columns.name: Trespasser Inuries (Subtotal)
columns.description: The count of injuries to trespassers in the event. A trespasser is any person in an area of transit property not intended for public use (i.e., an unauthorized area). Note that trespassers are also counted in the respective person type column to describe the action of the trespasser at the time of the event.

columns.name: People Waiting or Leaving Injuries
columns.description: Count of Person waiting / leaving from transit Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: People Waiting or Leaving Fatalities
columns.description: Count of Person waiting / leaving from transit fatalities for the given event.

columns.name: Multiple Locations List
columns.description: A list of event locations in "Act of God" events.

columns.name: Pedestrian in Crosswalk Injuries
columns.description: Count of Pedestrian: in crosswalk Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Evacuation
columns.description: Identifies if persons were evacuated from a transit facility or vehicle (True) or not (False) during the event.

columns.name: Pedestrian Crossing Tracks Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Pedestrian: person crossing tracks Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Pedestrian Walking Along Tracks Injuries
columns.description: Count of Pedestrian: person walking along tracks Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Pedestrian Walking Along Tracks Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Pedestrian: person walking along tracks Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Event Time
columns.description: The actual time of day the event occurred.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: The geographic coordinate that specifies the north–south position of a point on the Earth's surface. NTD requires a minimum of 4 decimal places, and if 7 decimals are not provided, the NTD systems fills to 7 decimals.

columns.name: Substantial Property Damage Type
columns.description: When a value is reported, this indicates Substantial Damage, which is defined as damage to transit or non-transit property including vehicles, facilities, equipment, rolling stock, or infrastructure that disrupts the operations of the rail transit agency and adversely affects the structural strength, performance, or operating characteristics of the property, requiring towing, rescue, on-site maintenance, or immediate removal prior to safe operation.

columns.name: Event Category
columns.description: Overall category of the event type

columns.name: Path Condition
columns.description: Identifies the pathway (roadway) conditions for a non-rail collision.

columns.name: Track Configuration
columns.description: Identifies the railway configuration at the scene for derailments.

columns.name: Road Configuration
columns.description: Identifies the roadway configuration at the scene of non-rail collision.

columns.name: Trespasser Serious Injuries (Subtotal)
columns.description: The count of serious injuries to trespassers in the event. A trespasser is any person in an area of transit property not intended for public use (i.e., an unauthorized area). Note that trespassers are also counted in the respective person type column to describe the action of the trespasser at the time of the event.

columns.name: Other Injuries
columns.description: Count of Other Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Manufacturer
columns.description: Identifies the manufacturer of the transit vehicle for collisions.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Rider Injuries
columns.description: Count of Transit vehicle rider Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Operator Injuries
columns.description: Count of Transit vehicle operator Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Non-Transit Vehicle Type List
columns.description: A list of vehicle types for non-transit vehicles involved in the event.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Type
columns.description: Identifies the form of passenger conveyance used for revenue operations.

columns.name: Other Transit Vehicle Action Description
columns.description: Text explanation from the "Please Describe" box used when "Other" is selected for the Transit Vehicle Action.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Operator Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Transit vehicle operator Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Non-Transit Vehicle Action List
columns.description: Identifies the other vehicle action at the time of the collision or derailment.

columns.name: Self Evacuation (Y/N)
columns.description: Identifies a self-evacuation, which occurs when people vacate transit property without direction from transit personnel or another authority.

columns.name: Transit Vehicle Action
columns.description: Identifies the non-rail, ferry, or rail vehicle action at the time of the collision or derailment.

columns.name: Other Rail Grade Crossing Comment
columns.description: Details for the grade crossing control device.

columns.name: Towed (Y/N)
columns.description: Identifier of collisions involving towing away from the scene for a transit roadway vehicle or other non-transit roadway vehicle that incurred disabling damage as a result of a collision.

columns.name: Event Date
columns.description: The date on which the event occurred.

columns.name: Pedestrian Not in Crosswalk Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Pedestrian: not in crosswalk Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Safety/Security
columns.description: Identifier of a Safety (SFT) or (Security Event). [Safety Event: A collision, derailment, fire, hazardous material spill, act of nature (Act of God), evacuation, or OSONOC occurring on transit right-of-way, in a transit revenue facility, in a transit maintenance facility, or involving a transit revenue vehicle and meeting established NTD thresholds.] OR [Security event: An occurrence of a bomb threat, bombing, arson, hijacking, sabotage, cyber security event, assault, robbery, rape, burglary, suicide, attempted suicide (not involving a transit vehicle), larceny, theft, vandalism, homicide, CBR (chemical/biological/radiological) or nuclear release, or other event.]

columns.name: Runaway Train Flag
columns.description: Identifier of events involving a runaway train.  [A train which is no longer under the control of a driver regardless of whether the operator is physically on the vehicle at the time. This is limited to revenue vehicles.]

columns.name: Intersection Control Device
columns.description: Identifies the intersection control device located at the intersection for a non-rail collision or rail collision.

columns.name: Rail/Bus/Ferry
columns.description: Groups modes based on whether they are:  Rail, Running Non-Rail Surface Transportation, or Ferryboat.

columns.name: Pedestrian Not in Crosswalk Fatalities
columns.description: Count of Pedestrian: not in crosswalk fatalities for the given event.

columns.name: Evacuation to Right-of-Way
columns.description: Identifies if the evacuation reported was to a controlled right-of-way (True) nor not (False).

columns.name: Location Type
columns.description: Location of the event

columns.name: Person List
columns.description: For each injury or fatality in an event, the following information is supplied separated by semicolons: I (Injury) or F (Fatality), H (Homicide or Assault) or  S (Suicide or Attempted Suicide), T (if the person was trespassing) or U (if the trespassing status is unknown), Person Type, Age Group, Gender, Y (if medical transport was required)

columns.name: Evacuation Location
columns.description: Identifies what was evacuated.

columns.name: Lighting
columns.description: Identifies the indoor or outdoor lighting conditions at the time of the collision or derailment.

columns.name: Other Event Type Description
columns.description: Text explanation from the 'Please describe" box used when "Other Security Event " is selected

columns.name: Fire Fuel
columns.description: For vehicle fires, identifies the type of fuel used to power the vehicle.

columns.name: Non-Operator Transit Employee Injuries
columns.description: Count of Transit employee or contractor Injuries for the given event. Includes "Other Transit Staff" inside the vehicle. Does not include vehicle operators.

columns.name: Current Condition
columns.description: Identifies the water current conditions at the time of the ferryboat collision

columns.name: Transit Worker Type
columns.description: Indicates whether the person assaulted is a transit operator, other transit worker, not a transit worker, or not specified. Only applies to assaults.

columns.name: Collision With
columns.description: Identifies what the transit vehicle collided with: Motor Vehicle, Non-Transit Motor Vehicle, Non-Rail Transit Vehicle, Rail Transit Vehicle/Rail Passenger Train – Revenue Service, Rail Transit Vehicle/Rail Passenger Train – Not in Revenue Service, Rail Transit Vehicle/Rail Transit Maintenance or Service Vehicle on the rail fixed guideway, Person, Animal, Fixed Object, Transit vehicle, Vessel, Dock/Terminal center, Non-revenue vehicle, Other.

columns.name: Pedestrian Not in Crosswalk Injuries
columns.description: Count of Pedestrian: not in crosswalk Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Derailment Type
columns.description: Identifies the type of rail derailment.

columns.name: Bicyclist Fatalities
columns.description: Count of Pedestrian: bicyclist fatalities for the given event.

columns.name: Mode
columns.description: Abbreviation of mode name.

columns.name: Tide
columns.description: Identifies the tide conditions at the time of the ferryboat collision.

columns.name: Property Damage
columns.description: Non-rail mode: The estimated dollar value of all property that is damaged in a Reportable Event. This includes transit-owned property and other vehicles and property involved in the event that are not owned by the transit agency (excludes personal property such as cell phones and computers). Property damage also includes the cost of clearing wreckage.

columns.name: Non-Operator Transit Employee Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Transit employee or contractor Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The calendar year in which the event occurred.

columns.name: HazMat Type
columns.description: Identifies the type of substance that caused the Hazardous Material Spill. [The spill or release of any amount of hazardous material that creates an imminent danger to life, health, or the environment and requires special attention be given to clean up the material. ]

columns.name: Event Description
columns.description: A 2000 character field used to describe the details of the event.

columns.name: Occupant of Other Vehicle Fatalities
columns.description: Count of Occupant of other vehicle fatalities for the given event.

columns.name: Right of Way Condition
columns.description: Identifies the right-of-way (ROW) conditions for non-rail or rail collision or rail derailment.

columns.name: Revenue Vehicle Identifier List
columns.description: A list of transit revenue vehicle fleet IDs with which to identify the transit vehicles involved in the event. Please find details about the transit vehicle fleet using the most recent Revenue Vehicle Inventory from https://transit.dot.gov/ntd/ntd-data.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: The geographic coordinate that specifies the east–west position of a point on the Earth's surface.NTD requires a minimum of 4 decimal places, and if 7 decimals are not provided, the NTD systems fills to 7 decimals.

columns.name: Bicyclist Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Pedestrian: bicyclist Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Transit (Y/N)
columns.description: Identifies the number of transit (Y) and non-transit (N) vehicles involved in the event.

columns.name: People Waiting or Leaving Serious Injuries
columns.description: Count of serious Person waiting / leaving from transit Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: A five-digit identifying number for each agency used in the current NTD system.

columns.name: Intentional (Y/N)
columns.description: Identifies if the security event was intentional or not,

columns.name: Fuel Type

columns.name: Service Stop Control Device
columns.description: Identifies the service stop control device located at the service stop for a rail collision.

columns.description: Count of Other worker (e.g.,commercial worker / utilites worker / etc.) Injuries for the given event.

columns.name: Agency

columns.name: Vehicle Speed
columns.description: Identifies the actual or estimated speed at the time of the collision or derailment.

columns.name: Approximate Address
columns.description: Identifies the exact or approximate address or location of the event.

columns.name: Total Fatalities

columns.name: Other Weather Comment
columns.description: Details for the weather at the time of the event.

columns.name: Other Worker Fatalities
columns.description: Count of Other worker (e.g.,commercial worker / utilites worker / etc.) fatalities for the given event.

columns.name: LatLon

columns.name: Mode Name

columns.description: Total number of involved injuries as a result of the event.

columns.name: Primary UZA UACE Code
columns.description: The Census Bereau's Urban Area Census Code (UACE). This five-digit code uniquely identifies an urban area and remains unchanged from census to census.

columns.name: Evacuation Comment
columns.description: The details of the evacuation.  [A reportable evacuation is a condition that occurs when persons depart from transit vehicles or facilities.  Evacuations to a location that may put passengers or patrons in imminent danger (such as controlled rail right-of-way) must also be reported.]

description: This is a list of all Major Safety and Security Events from January of 2014 to the most recently published data within the Federal Transit Administration's major event time series: https://www.transit.dot.gov/ntd/data-product/safety-security-major-only-time-series-data Monthly Safety and Security data are released to this dataset within three months of being submitted to the NTD. Generally, there will be at least 90-day lag between when the event occurs and when it is included in this data set.

description: A highway-rail crossing is a location where a public highway, road, street, or private roadway, including associated sidewalks and pathways, crosses one or more railroad tracks. All such crossings are reported by railroads and state Department of Transportations to the FRA on Form FRA F 6180.71 U.S. DOT Crossing Inventory Form. Each location has an assigned U.S. DOT Grade Crossing Identification Number, or crossing ID. This dataset is the source dataset and contains raw data values. It replaced

description: Data summarized by city, includes the number of city-pair markets in the top 1,000 in either comparison period that involve each city, the number of passengers traveling to and from each city, the average fare, average fare per mile (yield), and average distance traveled. All records are aggregated as directionless city pair markets. All traffic traveling in both directions is added together. https://www.transportation.gov/policy/aviation-policy/competition-data-analysis/research-reports

name: Advanced Messaging Concept Development Basic Safety Message
provider: U.S. Department of Transportation’s (USDOT) Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Joint Program Office (JPO) -- Recommended citation: "Advanced Messaging Concept Development Team. (2016). Advanced Messaging Concept Development Basic Safety Message. [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1504488"

columns.name: Transmission State
columns.description:
The state of the vehicle transmission known to the OBU at the time the BSM was generated.
 		0 = Neutral
 		1 = Park
 		2 = Forward
 		3 = Reverse
 		4 = Reserved
 		5 = Reserved
 		6 = Reserved
 		7 = Unavailable

columns.name: Longitudinal Acceleration
columns.description: The acceleration along the X axis or the vehicle's direction of travel, which is generally in parallel with a front to rear centerline. Expressed in meters per second squared where negative values indicate deceleration, and possible braking action.

columns.name: Lateral Acceleration
columns.description: The acceleration along the Y axis or perpendicular to the vehicle's general direction of travel in parallel with a left-to-right centerline. Expressed in meters per second squared where negative values indicate the vehicle is accelerating to left.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: The geographic latitude position as determined by the OBU GPS unit when the BSM was generated, expressed as a GPS coordinate.

columns.name: Length
columns.description: The length of the vehicle expressed in centimeters.

columns.name: Width
columns.description: The width of the vehicle expressed in centimeters.

columns.name: Mode of Transmission
columns.description:
This field contains an indication of the mode of transmission used to transmit the message (DSRC or Cellular) from the OBU to the VCC Cloud server.
If received via DSRC:
	1 through 114 = The ID of the RSU used to forward the message to the server 
If received via cellular: 
	999999 = Cellular

columns.name: Time Received
columns.description: This field contains the time the BSM was received by the VCC Cloud server in milliseconds UTC time.

columns.name: Brake Applied Status
columns.description:
A bit field representing a binary indication of whether brakes are being applied at each wheel. A component of the brake system status data frame.
	0000 = All off
	0001 = Left front active
	0010 = Left rear active
	0100 = Right front active
	1000 = Right rear active
	1111 = Brake is active

columns.name: Test No.
columns.description: The AMCD test number the data was collected during.

columns.name: Time Sent
columns.description: This field contains the time the message was sent from the OBU to the VCC Cloud server in milliseconds UTC time.

columns.name: OBU ID
columns.description: This field contains the ID of the OBU that generated and sent the message for the current communication sequence.

columns.name: Heading
columns.description: The heading of the vehicle expressed in degrees (0-360) where zero represents “North” and positive values express a heading values in a clockwise direction from “North.”

columns.name: Speed
columns.description: The vehicle speed in meters per second.

columns.name: Elevation
columns.description: The elevation of the vehicle in meters above mean sea level as determined by the OBU GPS when the BSM was generated.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Column to generate visualization

columns.name: ABS Active
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s ABS, where the ABS was detected to be active. 
	0 = Unavailable
	1 = Off
	2 = On
	3 = Engaged

columns.name: Yaw Rate
columns.description: A signed value representing degrees rotation per second of the vehicle around its vertical axis. Positive values indicate clockwise rotation of the vehicle around its vertical axis.

columns.name: Steering Wheel Angle
columns.description: The angle of the driver’s steering wheel, expressed in degrees of rotation from center where positive values represent rotation in the clockwise direction and negative values represent rotation in the counterclockwise direction.

columns.name: Stability Control Status
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s stability control system. If the vehicle is equipped with an SCS, the element reports whether the system was in an Off or On state.
	0 = Unavailable
	1 = Off
	2 = On

columns.name: Traction Control State
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s traction system. If the vehicle is equipped with a TCS, the element reports whether the system was in an Off, On, or Engaged state.
	0 = Unavailable
	1 = Off
	2 = On
	3 = Engaged

columns.name: Vertical Acceleration
columns.description: The signed vertical acceleration of the vehicle along the vertical axis in meters per second squared where positive values indicate upward (opposite of gravity) acceleration.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: The geographic longitude position as determined by the OBU GPS unit when the BSM was generated expressed as a GPS coordinate.

description: Contains all Basic Safety Messages (BSMs) collected during the Advanced Messaging Concept Development (AMCD) field testing program. For this project, all of the Part I BSM message fields were populated. Additional data fields were also added to the row to identify sender, time of communication, mode of communication, etc., allowing the consumer of this data set to accurately track messages through the system. All BSMs are generated by OBUs and ultimately received by the VCC Cloud server.

description: The National Highway Construction Cost Index (NHCCI) is a price index that can be used both to track price changes associated with highway construction costs, and to convert current dollar expenditures on highway construction to real or constant dollar expenditures. This dataset contains the quarterly NHCCI estimates as well as the Seasonally Adjusted NHCCI and Component Contributions to Changes in NHCCI. Visit https://www.fhwa.dot.gov/policy/otps/nhcci/ for more information regarding the NHCCI.

columns.name: Train Hours
columns.description: The hours that trains travel while in revenue service plus deadhead hours. Train hours include:  • Revenue service  • Deadhead  • Layover/recovery time  Train hours exclude:  • Hours for charter service  • Operator training  • Vehicle maintenance testing

columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Unlinked Passenger Trips (UPT)
columns.description: The number of passengers who board public transportation vehicles. Passengers are counted each time they board a vehicle no matter how many vehicles they use to travel from their origin to their destination.
