columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Passengers per Hour Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Train Miles
columns.description: The miles that trains travel while in revenue service (actual vehicle revenue miles (VRM)) plus deadhead miles. Train miles include:  • Revenue service  • Deadhead  Train miles exclude:  • Miles for charter services  • Operator training  • Vehicle maintenance testing

columns.name: Vehicle Revenue Hours Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Vehicle Hours Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Train Revenue Hours
columns.description: The hours that trains travel while in revenue service. Train revenue hours include:  • Revenue service  • Layover/recovery time  Train revenue hours exclude:  • Deadhead  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing  • Charter services

columns.name: Unlinked Passenger Trips Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Passenger Miles Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Train Deadhead Hours
columns.description: The hours that a train travels when out of revenue service. Deadhead includes:  • Leaving or returning to the garage or yard facility  • Changing routes  • When there is no expectation of carrying revenue passengers  However, deadhead does not include:  • Charter service  • School bus service  • Travel to fueling facilities  • Travel to maintenance facilities  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing

columns.name: Train Deadhead Miles
columns.description: The miles that a train travels when out of revenue service. Deadhead includes:  • Leaving or returning to the garage or yard facility  • Changing routes  • When there is no expectation of carrying revenue passengers  However, deadhead does not include:  • Charter service  • School bus service  • Travel to fueling facilities  • Travel to maintenance facilities  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing

columns.name: Train Revenue Miles
columns.description: The miles that trains travel while in revenue service. Train revenue miles include:  • Revenue service  Train revenue miles exclude:  • Deadhead  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing  • Charter services

columns.name: Charter Service Hours
columns.description: Hours run in charter service.

columns.name: Deadhead Hours Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Train Miles Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Average Passenger Trip Length (APTL)
columns.description: The average distance ridden by each passenger in a single trip, computed as passenger miles traveled (PMT) divided by unlinked passenger trips (UPT). May be determined by sampling, or calculated based on actual data.

columns.name: Service Area Sq Miles
columns.description: The agency's service area's size in square miles.

columns.name: ADA UPT
columns.description: Unlinked Passenger Trips embarked per stipulations of the Americans with Disabilities Act.

columns.name: UZA Population
columns.description: The population of the urbanized area primarily served by the agency.

columns.name: UZA Area Sq Miles
columns.description: The agency's Urbanized Area's size in square miles.

columns.name: UZA Name
columns.description: The name of the agency's Urbanized Area.

columns.name: Time Service Ends
columns.description: The time at which a particular mode/type of service ends revenue service, presented in a 24-hour format.

columns.name: Actual Vehicle/Passenger Car Miles
columns.description: The miles that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service (actual vehicle revenue miles (VRM)) plus deadhead miles. Actual vehicle miles include:  • Revenue service  • Deadhead  Actual vehicle miles exclude:  • Miles for charter services  • School bus service  • Operator training  • Vehicle maintenance testing

columns.name: Actual Vehicle/Passenger Car Revenue Miles
columns.description: The miles that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service. Vehicle revenue miles (VRM) include:  • Revenue service  Actual vehicle revenue miles exclude:  • Deadhead  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing  • School bus and charter services

columns.name: Time Period
columns.description: The time period for which data was collected.

columns.name: Actual Vehicle/ Passenger Deadhead Miles
columns.description: The miles that a vehicle/passenger car travels when out of revenue service. Deadhead includes:  • Leaving or returning to the garage or yard facility  • Changing routes  • When there is no expectation of carrying revenue passengers  However, deadhead does not include:  • Charter service  • School bus service  • Travel to fueling facilities  • Travel to maintenance facilities  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing

columns.name: BRT Non-Statutory Mixed Traffic Miles
columns.description: Miles of roadway for Bus Rapid Transit modes which, while part of bus rapid transit system routes, are not considered fixed guideway for federal funding allocation purposes. These are rare exceptions resulting from FTA’s review of each BRT system.

columns.name: Sponsored Service UPT
columns.description: Unlinked Passenger Trips embarked under sponsored service.

columns.name: Deadhead Miles Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: UACE Code
columns.description: UACE Code remains consistent across census years.

columns.name: Scheduled Vehicle/Passenger Car Revenue Miles
columns.description: The total service scheduled to be provided for picking up and discharging passengers. Scheduled service is computed from internal transit agency planning documents (e.g., run paddles, trip tickets, and public timetables). Scheduled service excludes special additional services.

columns.name: Directional Route Miles
columns.description: The mileage in each direction over which public transportation vehicles travel while in revenue service. Directional route miles (DRM) are:  •   A measure of the route path over a facility or roadway, not the service carried on the facility; e.g., number of routes, vehicles, or vehicle revenue miles. •   Computed with regard to direction of service, but without regard to the number of traffic lanes or rail tracks existing in the right-of-way (ROW). Directional route miles (DRM) do not include staging or storage areas at the beginning or end of a route.

columns.name: Actual Vehicle/Passenger Car Deadhead Hours
columns.description: The hours that a vehicle/passenger car travels when out of revenue service. Deadhead includes:  • Leaving or returning to the garage or yard facility  • Changing routes  • When there is no expectation of carrying revenue passengers  However, deadhead does not include:  • Charter service  • School bus service  • Travel to fueling facilities  • Travel to maintenance facilities  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing

columns.name: School Bus Hours
columns.description: Hours run in school bus service.

columns.name: Days Of Service Operated
columns.description: Days of revenue service within the fiscal year.

columns.name: Days Not Operated - Strikes
columns.description: Days of revenue service not operated due to strikes.

columns.name: Days Not Operated - Emergencies
columns.description: Days of revenue service not operated due to emergencies.

columns.name: Mixed Traffic Right-of-Way Miles
columns.description: Miles of roadway other than exclusive and controlled access rights-of-way (ROW) used for transit operations that are mixed with pedestrian and/or vehicle traffic. Does not include guideways that have only grade crossings with vehicular traffic.

columns.name: Mode VOMS
columns.description: The number of revenue vehicles operated by the given mode and type of service to meet the annual maximum service requirement. This is the revenue vehicle count during the peak season of the year; on the week and day that maximum service is provided. Vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) exclude atypical days and one-time special events.

columns.name: Directional Route Miles Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Type Of Service
columns.description: Describes how public transportation services are provided by the transit agency: directly operated (DO) or purchased transportation (PT) services.

columns.name: Passenger Miles Traveled
columns.description: The cumulative sum of the distances ridden by each passenger.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: Average Revenue Speed Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Trains in Operation Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Mode VOMS Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Train Revenue Miles Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Train Revenue Hours Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Passengers per Vehicle Revenue Hour
columns.description: The average number of passengers to board a vehicle/passenger car in one hour of service. For trains, this applies to passengers per hour on a single train car.

columns.name: Mode
columns.description: A system for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way (ROW), technology and operational features.

columns.name: Agency VOMS
columns.description: The number of revenue vehicles operated across the whole agency to meet the annual maximum service requirement. This is the revenue vehicle count during the peak season of the year; on the week and day that maximum service is provided. Vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) exclude atypical days and one-time special events.

columns.name: Actual Vehicle/Passenger Car Revenue Hours
columns.description: The hours that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service. Vehicle revenue hours (VRH) include:  • Revenue service  • Layover/recovery time  Actual vehicle revenue hours exclude:  • Deadhead  • Operator training  • Maintenance testing  • School bus and charter services

columns.name: Actual Vehicles/ Passenger Car Hours
columns.description: The hours that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service plus deadhead hours. Actual vehicle hours include:  • Revenue service  • Deadhead  • Layover/recovery time  Actual vehicle hours exclude:  • Hours for charter service  • School bus service  • Operator training  • Vehicle maintenance testing

columns.name: State
columns.description: The state in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: Time Service Begins
columns.description: The time at which a particular mode/type of service begins revenue service, presented in a 24-hour format.

columns.name: Trains In Operation
columns.description: The maximum number of trains actually operated to provide service during the year.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: A five-digit identifying number for each agency used in the current NTD system.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: The type of NTD report that the agency completed this year.

columns.name: Mode Name
columns.description: A system for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way (ROW), technology and operational features.

columns.name: Organization Type
columns.description: Description of the agency's legal entity.

description: This represents the Service data reported to the National Transit Database by transit agencies in the 2022, 2023, and 2024 report years. In versions of the data tables from before 2014, you can find data on service in the file called "Transit Operating Statistics: Service Supplied and Consumed." If you have any other questions about this table, please contact the NTD Help Desk at NTDHelp@dot.gov.

name: Department of Transportation Inventory of Artificial Intelligence Use Cases

columns.name: Appeal Process Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether there is an established appeal process for this AI use case in the event that an impacted individual would like to appeal or contest the AI system’s outcome, consistent with Section 4(b)(vi) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.
a) Yes, an appropriate appeal process has been established
b) Not applicable
c) Establishment of an appropriate appeal process is in-progress
d) Law, operational limitations, or governmentwide guidance precludes an opportunity for an individual to appeal
e) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Ongoing Monitoring Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether a process to conduct ongoing monitoring for the AI use case has been established, consistent with Section 4(b)(iii) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.
a) Yes, sufficient monitoring protocols have been established
b) Development of monitoring protocols is in-progress
c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Contractor Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether the system involved in the use case was developed, or is expected to be developed, exclusively with contracting resources, in-house, or a combination of both.
a) Purchased from a vendor
b) Developed in-house
c) Developed with both contracting and in-house resources

columns.name: Vendor Name
columns.description: This field identifies the vendor(s) involved in the use case’s development and/or maintenance.

columns.name: Operation Date
columns.description: This is date when the AI use case was fully operational or the pilot’s start date.  Format (MM/YYYY)

columns.name: Output Description
columns.description: This is description of what the AI system outputs, whether it’s a prediction, recommendation, decision, etc.

columns.name: Benefit Description
columns.description: This is description of the expected benefits and positive outcomes from the AI for an agency’s mission and/or the general public.

columns.name: Operator Training Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether sufficient and periodic training for operators of the AI use case has been established, consistent with Section 4(b)(iv) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.

a) Yes, sufficient and periodic training has been established
b) Establishment of sufficient and periodic training is in-progress
c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Potential Impact Description
columns.description: Identification of the reasonably foreseeable impacts from using the AI use case, to include impacts on the privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties of the public.

columns.name: Problem Description
columns.description: This field describes the problem AI intended to solve.

columns.name: AI Classification
columns.description: The classification of AI technology that most closely aligns with the AI use case. Note that an AI use case may rely on multiple classifications; however, agencies should select the choice that most closely aligns.  Values for this field are: Generative AI, Agentic AI, Classical/Predictive Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Reinforcement Learning, Other

columns.name: High Impact Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether the AI use case is high-impact, as defined in Section 5 of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.
a) High-impact
b) Presumed high-impact, but determined not high-impact
c) Not high-impact

columns.name: End User Feedback Description
columns.description: This field describes what steps the agency has taken to consult and incorporate feedback from end users of this AI use case and the public, consistent with Section 4(b)(vii) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.

columns.name: Predeployment Testing Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether pre-deployment testing has been conducted for the AI use case, consistent with Section 4(b)(i) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.
a) Yes
b) In-progress
c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Bureau
columns.description: Specific organization(s) within the agency responsible for the AI use case.

columns.name: Open Source Code URL
columns.description: This is the link to publicly available source code for the AI use case if available.

columns.name: Independent Review Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether an independent review of the AI use case has been conducted by an office, oversight board, or individual within the agency who has not been involved in the AI’s development.
a) Yes – by another appropriate agency office or reviewer not directly involved in the AI’s development
b) Yes – by an agency AI oversight board not directly involved in the AI’s development
c) Yes – by the CAIO
d) In-progress
e) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Public Reporting Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether the AI use case should be withheld from public reporting and the reason for why: 
a) No
b) Yes – agency has determined that there’s a risk to disclosure, such as a harm to an interest protected by a FOIA exemption
c) Yes – disclosure is prohibited by law
d) Other

columns.name: PII Indicator
columns.description: This field indicates if the AI use case involves personally identifiable information (PII), as defined in OMB Circular A-130.  Yes/No

columns.name: System Name
columns.description: The name of the system(s) associated with this AI use case, according to the ATO.

columns.name: Use Case Topic Area
columns.description: The topic area that most closely aligns with the AI use case.

columns.name: Enterprise Data Inventory URL
columns.description: This field is URL to publicly disclosed as an open government data asset in DOT Enterprise Data Inventory

columns.name: Use Case Name
columns.description: This field is title of the AI Use Case

columns.name: AI Impact Assessment Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether an AI impact assessment for the AI use case has been completed, consistent with Section 4(b)(ii) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.

a) Yes
b) In-Progress
c) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Custom Code Indicator
columns.description: This field indicates whether this AI project includes “custom-developed code,” as defined in OMB Memorandum M-25-21.  Yes/no

columns.name: Email Address
columns.description: Email address for additional questions on this AI Use Case.

columns.name: Demographic Variable Description
columns.description:
This field identifies the demographic features explicitly utilized in the AI use case’s data/model(s).  
a) Race/Ethnicity
b) Sex
c) Age
d) Religious Affiliation
e) Socioeconomic Status
f) Ability Status
g) Residency Status
h) Marital Status
i) Income
j) Employment Status
k) None of the above
l) Other

columns.name: High Impact Justification
columns.description: This field provides a justification for why the use case is determined to be not high-impact.  This question should only appear if “Presumed high-impact, but determined not high-impact” is selected for High Impact Indicator

columns.name: Use Case Identifier
columns.description: This field is unique identifier for AI use case. Use cases should maintain the same ID if included in previous year’s inventory.

columns.name: Development Stage
columns.description:
The AI use case’s current stage of development. 
a) Pre-deployment – The use case is in a development or acquisition status.
b) Pilot – The use case has been deployed in a limited test or pilot capacity.
c) Deployed – The use case is being actively authorized or utilized to support the functions or mission of an agency.
d) Retired – The use case was reported in the agency’s prior year’s inventory, but its development and/or use has since been discontinued.

columns.name: Fail Safe Indicator
columns.description:
This field indicates whether the AI use case has an appropriate fail-safe that minimizes the risk of significant harm, consistent with Section 4(b)(v) of the appendix to OMB Memorandum M-25-21.
a) Yes
b) Not applicable
c) In-progress
d) Agency CAIO has waived this minimum practice and reported such waiver to OMB

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: Agency responsible for the AI use case.

columns.name: Training Data Description
columns.description: This is general description of the data used in the AI’s design, development, training, testing, and operation. This should include a description of any data provided by the agency, whether the datasets are research datasets, publicly available, external, etc. and to the extent possible,

columns.name: ATO Indicator
columns.description: This field indicates whether the AI use case itself has an associated Authorization to Operate (ATO), or is part of another system that has an ATO.  Yes/No

columns.name: PIA URL
columns.description: This field indicate if the AI use case itself has an associated Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA), or is part of another system that has a PIA.

description: This dataset is a list of Department of Transportation (DOT) Artificial Intelligence (AI) use cases. The Advancing American AI Act, Executive Order (EO) 13960, Promoting the Use of Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence in the Federal Government, and Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Memorandum M-25-21, Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust, require Federal agencies to report on their use of artificial intelligence (AI).

name: Carrier - All With History
description: *Dataset* Records for all carriers/brokers/freight forwarders with active, inactive, or pending authorities (common or contract). It includes the DOT number and MC/FF/MX number for the carrier/broker/freight forwarder, along with company census data (e.g., types of authority, address, types of insurance on file, and amounts of insurance on file). See dataset attachment "FMCSA Dataset Description and Data Definitions - Select Datasets" for more information.

columns.name: Urbanized Area Population

columns.name: FG/NFG Reporting Method

columns.name: Fixed Guideway Vehicle Revenue Miles

columns.description: Indicates what data point the agency used to allocate data for each data point specific to each urbanized area.

columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service directional route miles greater than or equal to 7 years old for a particular UZA (see Directional Route Miles).

columns.name: Non-Fixed Guideway Vehicle Revenue Miles

columns.description: Agency allocation of vehicle revenue miles to the respective UZA (see Vehicle Revenue Hours)

columns.name: Total Unlinked Passenger Trips
columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service unlinked passenger trips to the respective UZA (see Unlinked Passenger Trips).

columns.name: Non-Fixed Guideway Passenger Miles
columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service non-fixed guideway passenger miles traveled to a particular UZA (see Passenger Miles Traveled and Fixed Guideway).

columns.name: Total Passenger Miles Traveled
columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service passenger miles traveled to the respective UZA (see Passenger Miles Traveled).

columns.name: Non-Fixed Guideway Operating Expenses
columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service non-fixed guideway operating expense to a particular UZA (see Vehicle Revenue Miles and Fixed Guideway).

columns.name: SGR FG Vehicle Revenue Miles
columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service vehicle revenue miles greater than or equal to 7 year old's for a particular UZA (see Vehicle Revenue Miles and Fixed Guideway).

columns.name: Urbanized Area Square Miles
columns.description: The square miles of the Urban Area served by the agency.

columns.name: SGR HIB Directional Route Miles
columns.description: Agency allocation of mode and type of service high intensity bus directional route miles greater than or equal to 7 year for a particular UZA (see Directional Route Miles).

description: Dataset containing all of the Federal Funding Allocation inputs submitted by reporting transit agencies to the National Transit Database in the 2022 - 2024 report years. This reflects the most recently published data within the Federal Transit Administration's NTD Data website.

columns.name: vehicle_impact

columns.description: The name of the individual or group responsible for the work zone data source.

columns.name: relationship_children
columns.description: Arrays of IDs indicating entities that are part of the road event with this relationship, such as a detour or piece of equipment.

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_contact_name
columns.description: The name of the individual or group responsible for the data feed.

columns.name: road_event_bbox
columns.description: GeoJSON Bounding Box on the coordinate range for the RoadEventFeature Feature.

columns.name: feed_bbox
columns.description: GeoJSON Bounding Box on the coordinate range for the WZDxFeed FeatureCollection.

columns.description: A URL where additional information on the LRS information and transformation information is stored.

columns.description: Describes the type of linear referencing system (LRS) used for the milepost measurements.

columns.name: data_source_update_frequency

columns.name: data_source_contact_email
columns.description: The email address of the individual or group responsible for the data source.

columns.description: The organization responsible for publishing the feed.

columns.description: Array of IDs indicating the first (can be multiple) road event in a sequence of road events by road_event_id.

columns.name: geometry_multipoint
columns.description: A coordinate pair or an array of coordinates. In either case, the first coordinate is the beginning point and the last coordinate is the ending point of the road event. Multi-Point

columns.name: geometry_linestring
columns.description: A coordinate pair or an array of coordinates. In either case, the first coordinate is the beginning point and the last coordinate is the ending point of the road event.

columns.name: lanes
columns.description: Stringified array of JSON objects identifying and describing individual lanes within a road event. Each lane JSON object can contain the following fields: “order” - The position of a lane in sequence on the roadway. This value is used as an index to indicate the order of all WZDx lanes provided for a road event; “type” - an indication of the type of lane or shoulder.); “status” - status of the lane for the traveling public; “lane_number” - the number assigned to the lane to help identify its position. Flexible, but usually used for regular, driveable lanes; “restrictions” - an array of lane_restriction JSON objects. Each lane_restriction JSON object contain the following fields: “restriction_type” - the type of restriction being enforced; “restriction_value” - the measure of the restriction type; “restriction_units” - units of measure for the restriction value. The “restriction_type” field of the lane_restriction JSON object is required. The “order”, “type”, and “status” fields of the lane JSON object are required.

columns.name: types_of_work
columns.description: Stringified array of JSON objects indicating the type of work being done in a road event, if applicable (e.g. typical work zones), as well as noting if the type of work will result in an architectural change to the roadway. Each JSON object can contain the following fields: “type_of_work_id” - ID identifying the types_of_work record; “type_name” - a high-level text description of the type of work being done; “is_architectural_change” - an optional boolean flag indicating whether the type of work will result in an architectural change to the roadway.

columns.name: update_date
columns.description: The UTC time and date when the activity or event was updated.

columns.name: creation_date
columns.description: The UTC time and date when the activity or event was created.

columns.name: description
columns.description: Short free text description of road event.

columns.name: restrictions
columns.description: One or more road restrictions applying to the work zone road segment associated with the work zone delimited by semicolons.

columns.name: reduced_speed_limit
columns.description: The reduced speed limit posted within the event space.

columns.name: workers_present
columns.description: A flag indicating that there are workers present in the event space.

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_update_date
columns.description: The UTC date and time when the data feed was last updated.

columns.name: data_source_update_date
columns.description: The UTC date and time when the data source was last updated.

columns.name: data_source_location_verify_method
columns.description: The method used to verify the accuracy of the location information.

columns.name: total_num_lanes
columns.description: The total number of lanes associated with the road event. This field has been deprecated as of WZDx v3.1.

columns.name: relationship_next
columns.description: Array of IDs indicating the next (can be multiple) road event in a sequence by road_event_id.

columns.name: data_source_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for the organization providing work zone data.

columns.name: ending_cross_street
columns.description: Name or number of the nearest cross street along the roadway where the event ends.

columns.name: road_names
columns.description: A list of publicly known names of the road on which the event occurs. This may include the road number designated by a jurisdiction such as a county, state or interstate (e.g. I-5, VT 133)

columns.name: end_date_accuracy
columns.description: A measure of how accurate the end Date Time is.

columns.name: start_date_accuracy
columns.description: A measure of how accurate the start Date Time is.

columns.name: beginning_cross_street
columns.description: Name or number of the nearest cross street along the roadway where the event begins.

columns.name: beginning_accuracy
columns.description: Indicates how the beginning coordinate was defined.

description: This dataset provides archived information on work zones in the state of Massachusetts in a tabular format. The ITS JPO has collections from 23 states including Massachusetts covering various parts of the time period from 10/2019 to 08/2024 depending on when the feed was active. The data is split into two archive files, the raw data contains the collection of .json or .geojson files exactly as they were on the individual state’s WZDx feed at the time of collection. The processed data is organize

name: Tampa CV Pilot Basic Safety Message (BSM) Sample
provider: United States Department of Transportation Intelligent Transportation Systems Joint Program Office (JPO) -- Recommended citation: "Tampa Hillsborough Expressway Authority. (2019). Tampa CV Pilot Basic Safety Message (BSM). [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1504502"

columns.name: coreData_brakes_auxBrakes
columns.description: Value is { "unavailable": null } for all Tampa data -- Auxiliary brake status - reflects the status of the auxiliary brakes (sometimes referred to as the parking brake) of the vehicle. The element can inform others that the vehicle is not equipped with auxiliary brakes or, if equipped, if the auxiliary brakes status is unavailable. If the vehicle is equipped with auxiliary brakes and the status is available, the element reports whether the auxiliary brakes are in a fully released (Off) state or in an engaged or in the process of being engaged (On) state. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_vse_events
columns.description: Column is not available for Tampa data -- BSM Part Two Vehicle Safety Extension Vehicle event flags - the sender's state with regard to a set of events, in bit string. For each event, the sender has the option to set the flag to 1 if the stated criteria are met, but it is not required to do so. The order in the bit string is: eventHazardLights, eventStopLineViolation, eventABSactivated, eventTractionControlLoss, eventStabilityControlactivated, eventHazardousMaterials, eventReserved1, eventHardBraking, eventLightsChanged, eventWipersChanged, eventFlatTire, eventDisabledVehicle, eventAirBagDeployment. The field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_vd_bumpers_rear
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension vehicle data rear bumper height - the height of the rear bumper of the vehicle or object (can also be used with trailers), in units of 0.01 meters from ground surface. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_cd_hpmstype
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension vehicle type - a type list (i.e., a classification list) of the vehicle in terms of overall size. The data element entries follow the definitions defined in the US DOT Highway Performance Monitoring System (HPMS). Many infrastructure roadway operators collect and classify data according to this list for regulatory reporting needs. Within the ITS industry and within the DSRC message set standards work, there are many similar lists of types for overlapping needs and uses. This field is based on the J2735 Standard. Use HPMS classes: "car" for light vehicles, "bus" for HART buses, "unknown" for TECO streetcars.

columns.name: coreData_brakes_scs
columns.description: Value is { "unavailable": null } for all Tampa data -- Stability control status - reflects the current state of the stability control system. The element can inform others that the vehicle is not equipped with stability control or, if equipped, if the stability control status is unavailable. If the vehicle is equipped with stability control and the status is available, the element reports whether the system is in an Off, On or Engaged state. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_wheelBrakes
columns.description: Value is "00000" for all Tampa data

columns.name: coreData_speed
columns.description: Vehicle speed, expressed in unsigned units of 0.02 meters per second. A value of 8191 shall be used when the speed is unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_angle
columns.description: The angle of the driver's steering wheel, in 1.5 degrees. A value of +126 is used for +189 degrees and beyond. A value of +127 is used for unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_accuracy_semiMinor
columns.description: Positional accuracy of semi-minor axis, in 5 cm. This is used to express the radius of the semi-minor axis of an ellipsoid representing the accuracy which can be expected from a GNSS system in 5cm steps, typically at a one sigma level of confidence. A value 254 is used for any value equal or greater than 12.70 m. A value of 255 is used for semi-minor axis unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_brakes_traction
columns.description: Value is { "unavailable": null } for all Tampa data -- Traction control status - reflects the status of the vehicle traction control system. The element can inform others that the vehicle is not equipped with traction control or, if equipped, if the traction control status is unavailable. If the vehicle is equipped with traction control and the status is available, the element reports whether the system is in an Off, On or Engaged state. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_position
columns.description: Geographic point of the vehicle, composed of (coreData_position_long, coreData_position_lat) in degrees. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: metadata_generatedAt_timeOfDay
columns.description: Time of day when the record was created, in unit of hour, based on the "metadata_generatedAt" field. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: coreData_transmission
columns.description: Value is { "unavailable": null } for Tampa data --Transmission state - current state of the vehicle transmission (neutral, park, forwardGears, reverseGears, reserved1, reserved2, reserved3, unavailable). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_vse_pp_confidence
columns.description: BSM Part Two Vehicle Safety Extension - Path Prediction confidence, in units of 0.5 percent. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_accuracy_semiMajor
columns.description: Positional accuracy of the semi-major axis, in 5 cm. This is used to express the radius (length) of the semi-major axis of an ellipsoid representing the accuracy which can be expected from a GNSS system in 5cm steps, typically at a one sigma level of confidence. A value 254 is used for any value equal or greater than 12.70 m. A value of 255 is used for semi-major axis unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_vse_lights
columns.description: BSM Part Two Vehicle Safety Extension Exterior Lights. The status of various exterior lights (when such data is available) encoded in a bit string which can be used to relate the current vehicle settings. The order in the bit string is: lowBeamHeadlightsOn, highBeamHeadlightsOn, leftTurnSignalOn, rightTurnSignalOn, hazardSignalOn, automaticLightControlOn, daytimeRunningLightsOn, fogLightOn, parkingLightsOn. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_recordGeneratedBy
columns.description: Source of the record, whether [OBU, RSU, TMC].

columns.name: dataType
columns.description: The data type.

columns.name: metadata_schemaVersion
columns.description: Version of the metadata schema.

columns.name: metadata_bsmSource
columns.description: Source of the BSM. Host vehicle = EV, Remote vehicle = RV.

columns.name: metadata_externalID
columns.description: External ID.

columns.name: coreData_brakes_brakeBoost
columns.description: Value is { "unavailable": null } for all Tampa data -- Auxiliary brake status - reflects the status of the auxiliary brakes (sometimes referred to as the parking brake) of the vehicle. The element can inform others that the vehicle is not equipped with auxiliary brakes or, if equipped, if the auxiliary brakes status is unavailable. If the vehicle is equipped with auxiliary brakes and the status is available, the element reports whether the auxiliary brakes are in a fully released (Off) state or in an engaged or in the process of being engaged (On) state. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_classification
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension basic vehicle class - common classification system to categorize DSRC- equipped devices for various cross-cutting uses. Use "transit-LocalBus" for HART buses, use "transit-FixedGuideway" for TECO streetcars. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: randomNum
columns.description: Random decimal number, to be used for random sampling of data within Socrata. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: metadata_RSUID
columns.description: Identifier of road side unit.

columns.name: coreData_brakes_abs
columns.description: Value is { "unavailable": null } for all Tampa data -- Anti-lock brake status - reflects the status of the vehicle ABS and can inform others that the vehicle is not equipped with ABS or, if equipped, if the ABS status is unavailable (0). If the vehicle is equipped with ABS and the status is available, the element reports whether the system is in an Off (1), On (2) or Engaged (3) state. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_size
columns.description: Vehicle size, representing the vehicle length and vehicle width in cm. This field is a stringified json object and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_vd_mass
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension vehicle data vehicle mass - estimated weight of the vehicle over a span of stepwise linear values. Values 000 to 080 are in steps of 50kg. Values 081 to 200 are in steps of 500kg. Values 201 to 253 are in steps of 2000kg. This provides a value range from zero to in excess of 170000 kg. The weight should reflect the current gross mass of vehicle and contents if known. The value 254 is used for weights above 170000 kg. The value 255 is used when the value is unknown or unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_id
columns.description: A 4 octet random device identifier, called the TemporaryID. When used for a mobile OBU device, this value will change periodically to ensure the overall anonymity of the vehicle, unlike a typical wireless or wired 802 device ID. Because this value is used as a means to identify the local vehicles that are interacting during an encounter, it is used in the message set. Other devices, such as infrastructure (RSUs), may have a fixed value for the temporary ID value. See also DE_StationID which is used in other deployment regions. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_vd_height
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension vehicle data vehicle height - The height of the vehicle, measured from the ground to the highest surface, excluding any antenna(s), and expressed in units of 5 cm. In cases of vehicles with adjustable ride heights, camper shells, and other devices which may cause the overall height to vary, the largest possible height will be used. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_cd_role
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension basic vehicle role - provides a means to indicate the current role that a DSRC device is playing. This is most commonly employed when a vehicle needs to take on another role in order to send certain DSRC message types. As an example, when a public safety vehicle such as a police car wishes to send a signal request message (SRM) to an intersection to request a preemption service, the vehicle takes on the role "police" from the below list in both the SRM message itself and also in the type of security CERT which is sent (the SSP in the CERT it used to identify the requester as being of type "police" and that they are allowed to send this message in this way). The BasicVehicleRole entry is often used and combined with other information about the requester as well, such as details of why the request is being made. Use role "transit"for both HART buses and for TECO streetcars. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_heading
columns.description: Heading of the sending device, expressed in unsigned units of 0.0125 degrees from North such that 28799 such degrees represent 359.9875 degrees. North shall be defined as the axis prescribed by the WGS-84 coordinate system and its reference ellipsoid. Headings "to the east" are defined as the positive direction. A value of 28800 shall be used when unavailable. This element indicates the direction of motion of the device. When the sending device is stopped and the trajectory (path) over which it traveled to reach that location is well known, the past heading may be used. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_suve_vd_bumpers_front
columns.description: BSM Part Two Supplemental Vehicle Extension vehicle data front bumper height - the height of the front bumper of the vehicle or object (can also be used with trailers), in units of 0.01 meters from ground surface. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_kind
columns.description: Metadata kind.

columns.name: coreData_lat
columns.description: Geographic latitude of the vehicle, expressed in units of 1/10th microdegrees and with reference to the horizontal datum then in use. The value 900000001 shall be used when unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_recordGeneratedAt
columns.description: Closest time to which the record was created, either signed or received by the generatedBy source in UTC format. This information is taken from the communication header.

columns.name: metadata_logFileName
columns.description: Name of the original file that deposited the message.

columns.name: part2_vse_pp_radiusofcurve
columns.description: BSM Part Two Vehicle Safety Extension - Path Prediction radius of curve, in units of 10cm. An estimate of the current trajectory of the sender. The value is represented as a first order of curvature approximation, as a circle with a radius R and an origin located at (0,R), where the x-axis is bore sight from the transmitting vehicle's perspective and normal to the vehicle's vertical axis. The vehicle's (x,y,z) coordinate frame follows the SAE convention. Radius R will be positive for curvatures to the right when observed from the transmitting vehicle's perspective. Radii shall be capped at a maximum value supported by the Path Prediction radius data type. Overflow of this data type shall be interpreted by the receiving vehicle as "a straight path" prediction. The radius can be derived from a number of sources including, but not limited to, map databases, rate sensors, vision systems, and global positioning. Straight path will use value of 32767. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_psid
columns.description: Provider Service Identifier. A number that identifies a service provided by an application. PSID is defined in IEEE Std 1609.12.

columns.name: coreData_msgCnt
columns.description: Message count data - used to provide a sequence number within a stream of messages with the same DSRCmsgID and from the same sender. A sender may initialize this element to any value in the range 0-127 when sending the first message with a given DSRCmsgID, or if the sender has changed identity (e.g. by changing its TemporaryID) since sending the most recent message with that DSRCmsgID. Depending on the application the sequence number may change with every message or may remain fixed during a stream of messages when the content within each message has not changed from the prior message sent. For this element, the value after 127 is zero. The receipt of a non-sequential MsgCount value (from the same sending device and message type) implies that one or more messages from that sending device may have been lost, unless MsgCount has been re-initialized due to an identity change. This field is based on the J2735 Standard

columns.name: coreData_accelset_lat
columns.description: Acceleration along the vehicle's lateral axis, in 0.01 m/s^2. Positive lateral axis is to the right side of the vehicle (facing forward). A value of 2000 is used for values greater than 2000, and a value of -2000 is used for values less than -2000. A value of 2001 is used for Unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: part2_vse_ph_crumbdata
columns.description: BSM Part Two Vehicle Safety Extension - Path History point list in stringified json format. The Path History defines a geometric path reflecting time-tagged vehicle movement over some period of time and/or distance. A sequence of Path History Points is used along with an initial position (and the GNSS status at that time) to create a set of straight line segments representing the path. For each Path History point, the lat-long offset units support units of 1/10th micro degrees of lat and long. The elevation offset units are in 10cm units. The time is expressed in units of 10 milliseconds. The PositionalAccuracy entry uses 3 elements to relate the pseudorange noise measured in the system. The heading and speed are not offset values, and follow the units defined in the ASN comments. All of these items are defined further in the relevant data entries. This field is a stringified array of json objects and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_accelset_yaw
columns.description: Yaw rotation rate of the vehicle about its vertical axis, in 0.01 degrees per second. Positive yaw is to the right (clockwise). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_accuracy_orientation
columns.description: Positional accuracy of the semi major axis orientation, in 360/65535 degree (0.0054932479 degree). This is used to orient the angle of the semi-major axis of an ellipsoid representing the accuracy which can be expected from a GNSS system with respect to the coordinate system. A value of 65535 is used for orientation unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_accelset_vert
columns.description: Acceleration value along the vehicle's vertical axis, in 0.02 G (0.02 G = 0.1962 m/s^2). Positive vertical "z" axis is downward with zero point at the bottom of the vehicle's tires. A value of +127 is used for ranges >= 2.54 G, and a value of -126 is used for ranges <= 2.52 G. A value of -127 is used for Unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_secMark
columns.description: Second Mark of the BSM - representing the milliseconds within a minute in units of milliseconds. The value of 65535 shall represent an unavailable value in the range of the minute. The values from 61000 to 65534 are reserved. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_elev
columns.description: The geographic position above or below the reference ellipsoid (typically WGS-84), in 10cm steps. The number represents an asymmetric range of positive and negative values. Any elevation higher than +6143.9 meters is represented as +61439. Any elevation lower than -409.5 meters is represented as -4095. If the sending device does not know its elevation, it shall encode the Elevation data element with -4096. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_accelset_long
columns.description: Acceleration value along the vehicle's longitudinal axis, in 0.01 m/s^2. Positive longitudinal axis is to the front of the vehicle. A value of 2000 is used for values greater than 2000, and a value of -2000 is used for values less than -2000. A value of 2001 is used for Unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: coreData_long
columns.description: Geographic longitude of the vehicle, expressed in units of 1/10th microdegrees and with reference to the horizontal datum then in use. The value 1800000001 shall be used when unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

tags: tampa connected vehicle pilot deployment (tampa cv pilot)
description: The Tampa CV Pilot generates data from the interaction between vehicles and between vehicles and infrastructure. This dataset consists of Basic Safety Messages (BSMs) generated by participant and public transportation vehicles onboard units (OBU) and transmitted to road-side units (RSU) located throughout the Tampa CV Pilot Study area. The full set of raw, BSM data from Tampa CV Pilot can be requested from the ITS DataHub Sandbox. The data fields follow SAE J2735 and J2945/1 standards and adopte

name: 2024 NTD Annual Data - Demand Response Geographic Area Coverage (Counties and Places)

columns.description: A unique identifier for a county.

columns.description: The level at which the Census-designated place is served: Wholly Served or Partially Served.

columns.name: Place Name
columns.description: The name of the Census-designated place (CDP) that is served by transit.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: Reporter Type will be based on where they operate and the reporting requirements associated with their agency. Much more discussion is provided in the NTD Data Publications Guide.  Agencies that receive Chapter 53 funds and own, operate, or manage capital assets in public transportation are also required to file an annual report, even if they do not receive §5307 or §5311 funds. Agencies that do not receive or benefit from FTA funding may elect to submit their data to the NTD as Voluntary Reporter but are still assigned a reporter type.

columns.name: State Name
columns.description: The name of the state in which the served place/county combination is located. Note that a place may lie in two states, but a county does not. Therefore, a place/county combination is unique to a given state.

columns.name: Mode
columns.description: A system for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way (ROW), technology, and operational features

columns.name: County Name
columns.description: The name of the county in which the served place is located.

columns.name: Place Geo ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for a place. A place will have the same Place Geo ID regardless of how many counties it lies in.

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: The agency name is the full legal name of the agency. If reporting is required under an FTA grant program, this must reflect the legal name of the funding recipient.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: FTA assigns each reporter a unique five-digit NTD Identification Number. The first digit of the NTD ID corresponds to the FTA Region where the reporter is located (e.g., 9#### indicates Region IX). The code will have a four-to-five digit prefix for any entity submitting the report on behalf of the reporter. For example, State Departments of Transportation (usually indicated as #R##) submit on behalf of their subrecipients.

description: This dataset details places and counties served by Demand Response (DR) modes for each applicable agency and type of service (TOS) reported to the National Transit Database for the 2024 report year. NTD Data Tables organize and summarize data from the 2024 National Transit Database in a manner that is more useful for quick reference and summary analysis. This data is a part of new reporting requirements as of 2023. Other datasets describing aspects of Demand Response Geographical Area Coverage c

name: InsHist - All With History
description: *Dataset* This dataset contains information on a carrier’s/broker’s/freight forwarder’s previous insurance policy(ies). This dataset contains the DOT number and docket number of the entity. Additionally, it contains the cancellation method (cancelled, replaced, name change, transferred), the type of policy, the policy number, and the effective and cancellation dates of the policy. All insurance information is related to the insurance policy either being cancelled, being replaced, or prior to a n

description: The WZDx Specification enables infrastructure owners and operators (IOOs) to make harmonized work zone data available for third party use. The intent is to make travel on public roads safer and more efficient through ubiquitous access to data on work zone activity. Specifically, the project aims to get data on work zones into vehicles to help automated driving systems (ADS) and human drivers navigate more safely. MCDOT leads the effort to aggregate and collect work zone data from the AZTech Regi

columns.name: Weblink Justification
columns.description: FTA allows agencies who are in the process of hosting GTFS files online to provide the GTFS data as a zip file. Please contact NTDHelp@dot.gov to request these files.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: Agency VOMS
columns.description: The number of revenue vehicles operated across the whole agency to meet the annual maximum service requirement. This is the revenue vehicle count during the peak season of the year; on the week and day that maximum service is provided. Vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) exclude atypical days and one-time special events.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: A five-digit identifying number for each agency used in the current NTD system.

columns.name: TOS
columns.description: Describes how public transportation services are provided by the transit agency: directly operated (DO) or purchased transportation (PT) services.

columns.description: Abbreviation of mode name

columns.name: UZA Name
columns.description: The name of the urbanized area primarily served by the agency. Urbanized areas are Census urban areas with a population of 50,000 or greater as of the last decennial Census.

columns.name: Date Validated
columns.description: The date that the data was last reviewed by FTA. Note: if this date is earlier than the Modified Date, the weblink has not yet been reviewed. If you encounter issues with a weblink that has been validated, please contact NTDHelp@dot.gov, as this may indicate an error with the host website.

columns.name: UACE Code
columns.description: UACE Code remains consistent across census years.

columns.name: Modified Date
columns.description: The date the weblink was last modified.

columns.name: Mode VOMS
columns.description: The number of revenue vehicles operated by the given mode and type of service to meet the annual maximum service requirement. This is the revenue vehicle count during the peak season of the year; on the week and day that maximum service is provided. Vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) exclude atypical days and one-time special events.

columns.name: Primary UZA Population
columns.description: The population of the Urbanized Area served by the agency.

columns.name: Certification Flag
columns.description: A "Y" in this column indicates that the weblink was certified by the transit agency's CEO or CEO representative. Rural Reporters do not submit a CEO Certification form; therefore this flag will be null for all agencies where Reporter Type = Rural Reporter.

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: The transit agency's name.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The state in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: Waived
columns.description: A "Y" in this column indicates that FTA waived the requirement to submit the GTFS weblink in Report Year 2023 at the agency's request.

columns.name: Mode Name
columns.description: A system for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way (ROW), technology and operational features.

columns.name: Weblink
columns.description: Weblink to the publicly hosted GTFS files for the respective transit agency. These files can be used in mapping and other geospatial visualization and analyses of fixed route transit services.

description: As of Report Year (RY) 2023, FTA requires that reporters with fixed route modes create and maintain a public domain general transit feed specification (GTFS) dataset that reflects their fixed route service. This specification allows for the mapping and other geospatial data visualization and analyses of key transit elements such as stops, routes, and trips. At least one GTFS weblink is provided by the transit agency for each fixed route bus mode and type of service. These include all Rail modes

description: A highway-rail crossing is a location where a public highway, road, street, or private roadway, including associated sidewalks and pathways, crosses one or more railroad tracks. All such crossings are reported by railroads and state Department of Transportations to the FRA on Form FRA F 6180.71 U.S. DOT Crossing Inventory Form. Each location has an assigned U.S. DOT Grade Crossing Identification Number, or crossing ID. This dataset is the source dataset and contains raw data values. It replaced

name: 2023 NTD Annual Data - Demand Response Geographic Area Coverage (Service Schedules)

columns.name: End Next Day Flag
columns.description: A flag that indicates whether a service ends the day after it begins (i.e., if it runs past midnight into the next day).

columns.name: TOS
columns.description: Modes of public transit that are purchased by the transit agency but reported by a separate entity.

columns.name: Service Begin Time
columns.description: The time at which service begins on the given day of the week.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: Reporter Type will be based on where they operate and the reporting requirements associated with their agency. Much more discussion is provided in the NTD Data Publications Guide.  Agencies that receive Chapter 53 funds and own, operate, or manage capital assets in public transportation are also required to file an annual report, even if they do not receive §5307 or §5311 funds. Agencies that do not receive or benefit from FTA funding may elect to submit their data to the NTD as Voluntary Reporter but are still assigned a reporter type.

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: The agency name is the full legal name of the agency. If reporting is required under an FTA grant program, this must reflect the legal name of the funding recipient.

columns.name: ADA Eligible Flag
columns.description: A flag that indicates whether some or all of the service is intended to meet the ADA complementary paratransit requirement for a fixed route service on a given day.

columns.name: Defined by Age/Disability Flag
columns.description: A flag that indicates whether some or all of the service is restricted to elderly, disabled, or low-income riders on a given day. This does not necessarily mean that the service is intended to meet the ADA complementary paratransit requirement for a fixed route service.

columns.name: Service End Time
columns.description: The time at which service ends on the given day of the week.

columns.name: Day of Week
columns.description: Name of the day of the week.

columns.name: General Population Flag
columns.description: A flag that indicates whether the general population is able to use the service.

columns.name: Mode
columns.description: A system for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way (ROW), technology, and operational features

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: FTA assigns each reporter a unique five-digit NTD Identification Number. The first digit of the NTD ID corresponds to the FTA Region where the reporter is located (e.g., 9#### indicates Region IX). The code will have a four-to-five digit prefix for any entity submitting the report on behalf of the reporter. For example, State Departments of Transportation (usually indicated as #R##) submit on behalf of their subrecipients.

description: This dataset details service schedules for Demand Response (DR) modes for each applicable agency and type of service (TOS) reported to the National Transit Database for Report Year 2023. NTD Data Tables organize and summarize data from the 2023 National Transit Database in a manner that is more useful for quick reference and summary analysis. This data is a part of new reporting requirements as of 2023. Other datasets describing aspects of Demand Response Geographical Area Coverage can be found

columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Locomotive with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Automated Guideway Vehicle
columns.description: Guided transit passenger vehicles operating under a fully automated system (no crew on transit units).

columns.name: Aerial Tramway with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Commuter Rail Self-Propelled Passenger Car
columns.description: Commuter rail (CR) passenger vehicles not requiring a separate locomotive for propulsion.

columns.name: Locomotive >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Vintage/Historic Trolley
columns.description: Vintage or antique rail cars typically originally manufactured before 1975. The vehicles are typically operated in mixed traffic right-of-way (ROW), but may also operate on exclusive or controlled access ROW.

columns.name: Steel Wheel Vehicles
columns.description: In rail systems, vehicles with the specially designed cast or forged steel, essentially cylindrical element that rolls on the rail, carries the weight, and provides guidance for rail vehicles. Steel wheel vehicles exclude vehicles that are equipped for both road (rubber tires) and rail.

columns.name: Cable Car with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Inclined Plane >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Automobiles
columns.description: Passenger cars, up to and including station wagons in size. Excludes minivans and anything larger.

columns.name: Ferryboat
columns.description: Passenger-carrying boats.

columns.name: Total with ULB Reported
columns.description: Total count of vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Trucks and Other Rubber Tire Vehicles
columns.description: A self-propelled, motor vehicle designed primarily for the transportation of property or special purpose equipment. It may consist of a chassis and body; a chassis, cab and body; or it may be of integral construction so that the body and chassis form a single unit. This vehicle category also includes pickup trucks, vans, SUVs, and minivans.

columns.name: Total Service Vehicles >= ULB
columns.description: Total count of service vehicles that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Streetcar
columns.description: A modern streetcar vehicle powered by an overhead catenary and intended for street running. Contrast with vintage trolley.

columns.name: Monorail
columns.description: Guided transit passenger vehicles operating on or suspended from a single rail, beam or tube.

columns.name: Total Service Vehicles
columns.description: Vehicles that indirectly deliver transit service, maintain revenue vehicles, and perform transit-oriented administrative activities. Agencies must report service vehicles for which they have capital replacement responsibility. Service vehicles must be self-propelled and either road-worthy or major pieces of construction equipment to be reportable to the NTD. Examples of reportable service vehicles include: automobiles used by supervisors or maintenance staff, wreckers, tow trucks, work trains, tampers, diggers, etc. Flatbed train cars, golf carts, and fork lifts are not considered reportable service vehicles.

columns.name: Vintage/Historic Trolley with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Primary UZA Population
columns.description: The population of the urbanized area primarily served by the agency.

columns.name: Commuter Rail Self-Propelled Passenger Car with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Locomotive
columns.description: Commuter rail (CR) vehicles used to pull or push CR passenger coaches. Locomotives do not carry passengers themselves.

columns.name: Commuter Rail Self-Propelled Passenger Car >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Commuter Rail Passenger Coach >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Commuter Rail Passenger Coach with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Commuter Rail Passenger Coach
columns.description: Commuter rail (CR) passenger vehicles not independently propelled and requiring one or more locomotives for propulsion.

columns.name: Light Rail Vehicle with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Automobile
columns.description: Passenger cars, up to and including station wagons in size. Excludes minivans and anything larger.

columns.name: SchoolBus with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Double Decker Bus
columns.description: High capacity buses with two levels of seating, one over the other, connected by one or more stairways. Total bus height is usually 13 to 14.5 feet, and typical passenger seating capacity ranges from 40 to 80 people.

columns.name: Light Rail Vehicle >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Light Rail Vehicle
columns.description: Rail cars employed in light rail mode with:  • Motive capability  • Usually driven by electric power taken from overhead lines  • Configured for passenger traffic  • Usually operating on exclusive rights-of-way (ROW)

columns.name: Heavy Rail Passenger Car >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Heavy Rail Passenger Car with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Heavy Rail Passenger Car
columns.description: Rail cars employed in heavy rail mode with:  • Motive capability  • Driven by electric power taken from overhead lines or electrified third rails  • Configured for passenger traffic  • Operated on exclusive right-of-way (ROW)

columns.name: Sport Utility Vehicle >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Ferryboat with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Aerial Tramway >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Sport Utility Vehicle
columns.description: A high-performance four-wheel drive car built on a truck chassis. A passenger vehicle combines the towing capacity of a pickup truck with the passenger-carrying space of a minivan or station wagon. Most Sport Utility Vehicles (SUVs) are designed with a roughly square cross-section, an engine compartment, a combined passenger and cargo compartment, and no dedicated trunk. Most mid-size and full-size SUVs have three rows of seats with a cargo area directly behind the last row of seats. Compact SUVs and mini SUVs may have five or fewer seats.

columns.name: Minivan >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Minivan with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Minivan
columns.description: A light duty vehicle having a typical seating capacity of up to seven passengers plus a driver. A minivan is smaller, lower and more streamlined than a full-sized van, but it is typically taller and has a higher floor than a passenger car. Minivans normally cannot accommodate standing passengers.

columns.name: Cutaway >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Cutaway with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Cutaway
columns.description: A vehicle that consists of a bus body that is mounted on the chassis of a van or light-duty truck. The original van or light-duty truck chassis may be reinforced or extended. Cutaways typically seat 15 or more passengers and may accommodate some standing passengers. The cutaway manufacturer differs from the chassis manufacturer.

columns.name: Van with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Ferryboat >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Inclined Plane
columns.description: Special type of passenger vehicles operating up and down slopes on rails via a cable mechanism.

columns.name: School Bus
columns.description: Passenger vehicles that are:  • Designed or used to carry more than ten passengers in addition to the driver; and  • Used primarily for the purpose of transporting pre-primary, primary, or secondary school students to such schools either from home or from such schools to home.

columns.name: Double Decker Bus with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Over-the-Road Bus >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Articulated Bus with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Articulated Bus
columns.description: Extra-long (usually 54 ft. to 60 ft.) buses with two connected passenger compartments. The rear body section is connected to the main body by a joint mechanism that allows the vehicles to bend when in operation for sharp turns and curves and yet have a continuous interior.

columns.name: Bus >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Bus with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Streetcar >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Bus
columns.description: Rubber-tired passenger vehicles powered by diesel, gasoline, battery, or alternative fuel engines contained within the vehicle. Vehicles in this category do not include articulated, double-decked, or school buses, or cutaway vans.

columns.name: Over-the-Road Bus
columns.description: A bus characterized by an elevated passenger deck located over a baggage compartment.

columns.name: School Bus >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: State
