columns.name: Total Revenue Vehicles >= ULB
columns.description: Total count of revenue vehicles that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Cable Car
columns.description: Streetcar type of passenger vehicles operating by means of an attachment to a moving cable located below the street surface and powered by engines or motors at a central location not onboard the vehicles.

columns.name: Monorail >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Automobile >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Monorail with ULB Reported
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have a reported Useful Life Benchmark.

columns.name: Van >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Report Year
columns.description: The year for which the data was reported.

columns.name: Agency VOMS
columns.description: The number of revenue vehicles operated across the whole agency to meet the annual maximum service requirement. This is the revenue vehicle count during the peak season of the year; on the week and day that maximum service is provided. Vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) exclude atypical days and one-time special events.

columns.name: Steel Wheel Vehicles >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Trolleybus
columns.description: Rubber-tired, electrically powered passenger vehicles operated on city streets drawing power from overhead lines with trolleys.

columns.name: Other

columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Automobiles >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

columns.name: Aerial Tramway
columns.description: Unpowered passenger vehicles suspended from a system of aerial cables and propelled by separate cables attached to the vehicle suspension system. Engines or motors at a central location, not onboard the vehicle, power the cable system.

columns.name: Trucks and Other Rubber Tire Vehicles >= ULB
columns.description: Count of vehicles in a given category that have met or exceeded their Useful Life Benchmark. Only vehicles with a reported Useful Life Benchmark are counted in this subset.

description: This dataset details vehicle types and ages for transit agencies reporting to the National Transit Database in the 2022, 2023, and 2024 report years. Vehicle types describe the vehicles employed in direct operation or support of transit service. NTD Data Tables organize and summarize data from the 2022 - 2024 National Transit Database in a manner that is more useful for quick reference and summary analysis. This dataset is based on the 2022 - 2024 Revenue Vehicle Inventory and Service Vehicle In

name: Tampa CV Pilot Traveler Information Message (TIM) Sample
provider: United States Department of Transportation Intelligent Transportation Systems Joint Program Office (JPO) -- Recommended citation: "Tampa Hillsborough Expressway Authority. (2019). Tampa CV Pilot Traveler Information Message (TIM). [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1504498"

columns.name: travelerdataframe_frameType
columns.description: The type of message to follow in the rest of the message frame structure (advisory or road sign). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_directionality
columns.description: Direction of use (forward, reverse, both, unavailable) - the allowed direction of travel on a street lane or path described by shape points. The presumed (default) direction is outward, away from the initial set of points. However, this data element can be used indicate a reverse direction or both directions as well as the original outward direction. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_name
columns.description: Human readable and recognizable name for the spatial areas it describes. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_anchor_lat
columns.description: Geographic latitude at start of speed recommendation, in units of 1/10th microdegrees. The value 900000001 shall be used when unavailable. This is redundant with road sign position. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerinformation_urlB
columns.description: A valid internet style URI / URL in the form of a text string which will form the base of a compound string which, when combined with the URL-Short data element, will link to the designated resource. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_generatedAt_timeOfDay
columns.description: Time of day when the record was created, in unit of hour, based on the "metadata_generatedAt" field. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: randomNum
columns.description: Random decimal number, to be used for random sampling of data within Socrata. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_position
columns.description: Geographic point at start of speed recommendation, composed of (travelerdataframe_msgId_long, travelerdataframe_msgId_lat) in degrees. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_startTime
columns.description: Start time of message - the number of elapsed minutes of the current year in the time system being used (typically UTC time). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_sspLocationRights
columns.description: SSP Index for locations. The ability to send TIM Part II regions information is controlled by this SSPIndex which links back to the sender's CERT. This is set to 0 in this dataset. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_sspTimRights
columns.description: SSP Index for TIM. The ability to send TIM is controlled by this SSPIndex which links back to the sender's CERT. This is set to 0 in this dataset. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_content_advisory_sequence
columns.description: Advisory ITIS warnings - ITIS codes interspersed with free text. The complete set of ITIS codes can be found in Volume Two of the J2540 Standard. This is a set of nearly 1,500 items which are used to encode common events and list items in ITS. This field is a stringified json object and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_laneWidth
columns.description: Width of a lane in cm. Maximum value for a lane is 327.67 meters in width. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerinformation_packetID
columns.description: Packet ID - a relatively unique value which can be used to connect to (link to) other supporting messages in other formats. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_furtherInfoId
columns.description: Further Info ID - a link number to other messages (described here and in other message set standards) which relate to the same event. Use zero when unknown or not present. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_long
columns.description: Geographic longitude at start of speed recommendation, in units of 1/10th microdegrees. The value 1800000001 shall be used when unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_content_exitService_sequence
columns.description: Roadside available services - contains ITIS codes interspersed with free text. This field is a stringified json object and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_lat
columns.description: Geographic latitude at start of speed recommendation, in units of 1/10th microdegrees. The value 900000001 shall be used when unavailable. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_viewAngle
columns.description: Vehicle direction of travel while facing active side of sign, in the format of bit string. Each bit 22.5 degree starting from North and moving Eastward (clockwise) as one bit. A value of noHeading means no bits set, while a value of allHeadings means all bits would be set. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerinformation_timeStamp
columns.description: The number of elapsed minutes of the current year in the time system being used (typically UTC time). It is typically used to provide a longer range time stamp indicating when a message was created. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_sspMsgRights2
columns.description: SSP Index for message content. The ability to send TIM Part III content information is controlled by this SSPIndex which links back to the sender's CERT. This is set to 0 in this dataset. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_sspMsgRights1
columns.description: SSP Index for message types. The ability to send TIM Part III message type information is controlled by this SSPIndex which links back to the sender's CERT. This is set to 0 in this dataset. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_generatedAt
columns.description: Closest time to which the record was created, either signed or received by the generatedBy source in UTC format. This information is taken from the communication header.

columns.name: metadata_psid
columns.description: Provider Service Identifier. A number that identifies a service provided by an application. PSID is defined in IEEE Std 1609.12.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_id_region
columns.description: Road regulator ID - a globally unique regional assignment value typically assigned to a regional DOT authority. The value zero shall be used for testing needs. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_desc_nodes
columns.description: Description of a system of paths. This field is a stringified array of json objects and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_schemaVersion
columns.description: Version of the metadata schema.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_id_id
columns.description: Road Segment ID - a unique mapping to the road segment in question within the Road Regular ID region of use during its period of assignment and use. Note that unlike intersection ID  values, this value can be reused by the region. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_duratonTime
columns.description: Duration of validity of this speed recommendation or wrong-way driver warning, in units of whole minutes, that a object persists for. A value of 32000 means that the object persists forever. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_content_workZone_sequence
columns.description: Work zone signs and directions - contains ITIS codes interspersed with free text. This field is a stringified json object and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_priority
columns.description: The relative importance of the sign, on a scale from zero (least important) to seven (most important). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_mutcdCode
columns.description: Tag for MUTCD code (none, regulatory, warning, maintenance, motoristService, guide, rec) or "generic sign". MUTCD has no appropriate code for an ERDW speed advisory or wrong-way driver warning. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_anchor_regional
columns.description: Regional extension of geographical path described. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_logFileName
columns.description: Name of the original file that deposited the message.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_closedPath
columns.description: Boolean field indicating if the geographical path in the TIM is closed or not - when true, last point closes to first. Assume that omitting element indicates closedPath == false. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_regional
columns.description: Regional extension of Traveler Information. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_elevation
columns.description: Elevation location at start of speed recommendation, in units of 10cm above or below the reference ellipsoid. Providing a range of -409.5 to +6143.9 meters. The value -4096 shall be used when Unknown is to be sent. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerinformation_msgCnt
columns.description: Message count, to be increased whenever the content of any TravelerDataFrame changes. The receipt of a non-sequential MsgCount value (from the same sending device and message type) implies that one or more messages from that sending device may have been lost, unless MsgCount has been re-initialized due to an identity change. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_kind
columns.description: Metadata kind.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_anchor_elevation
columns.description: Elevation at start of speed recommendation, in units of 10cm above or below the reference ellipsoid. Providing a range of -409.5 to +6143.9 meters. The value -4096 shall be used when Unknown is to be sent. This is redundant with road sign position. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_msgId_msgCrc
columns.description: Octet string used to provide a check sum.  This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_direction
columns.description: Heading slice - field of view over which the TIM geographical path applies. In the format of bit string. Each bit 22.5 degree starting from North and moving Eastward (clockwise) as one bit. A value of noHeading means no bits set, while a value of allHeadings means all bits would be set. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_anchor_long
columns.description: Geographic longitude at start of speed recommendation, in units of 1/10th microdegrees. The value 1800000001 shall be used when unavailable. This is redundant with road sign position. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_content_genericSign_sequence
columns.description: Generic MUTCD signs and directions - contains ITIS codes interspersed with free text. This field is a stringified json object and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_content_speedLimit_sequence
columns.description: Speed limits and cautions - contains ITIS codes interspersed with free text. This field is a stringified json object and is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_startYear
columns.description: Start Year of message. The year according to the Gregorian calendar date system. The value of zero shall represent an unknown value. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_RSUID
columns.description: Identifier of road side unit.

columns.name: travelerinformation_regional
columns.description: Regional extension of Traveler Information. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: travelerdataframe_desc_scale
columns.description: Scaling factor of geographical path described. The zoom value of zero implies a 1:1 scale (no zoom), while a larger value implies an increase by a power of X = 2^z, where z is the zoom scale. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_generatedBy
columns.description: Source of the record, whether [OBU, RSU, TMC].

columns.name: dataType
columns.description: The data type.

tags: category: traveler information and personal mobility
tampa connected vehicle pilot deployment (tampa cv pilot)
description: The Tampa CV Pilot generates data from the interaction between vehicles and between vehicles and infrastructure. This dataset consists of Traveler Information Messages (TIMs) transmitted by road-side units (RSU) located throughout the Tampa CV Pilot Study area. The full set of raw, TIM data from Tampa CV Pilot can be requested from the ITS DataHub Sandbox. The data fields follow a SAE J2735 TIM message structure to convey important traffic information to onboard units (OBU) of equipped vehicles.

name: 2023 NTD Annual Data - Demand Response Geographic Area Coverage (Counties and Places)

columns.name: Place Name
columns.description: The name of the Census-designated place (CDP) that is served by transit.

columns.name: TOS
columns.description: Modes of public transit that are purchased by the transit agency but reported by a separate entity.

columns.name: Level of Service
columns.description: The level at which the Census-designated place is served: Wholly Served or Partially Served.

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: The agency name is the full legal name of the agency. If reporting is required under an FTA grant program, this must reflect the legal name of the funding recipient.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: FTA assigns each reporter a unique five-digit NTD Identification Number. The first digit of the NTD ID corresponds to the FTA Region where the reporter is located (e.g., 9#### indicates Region IX). The code will have a four-to-five digit prefix for any entity submitting the report on behalf of the reporter. For example, State Departments of Transportation (usually indicated as #R##) submit on behalf of their subrecipients.

columns.name: Place_Geo_ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for a place. A place will have the same Place Geo ID regardless of how many counties it lies in.

columns.name: County_Geo_ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for a county.

columns.name: County Name
columns.description: The name of the county in which the served place is located.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: Reporter Type will be based on where they operate and the reporting requirements associated with their agency. Much more discussion is provided in the NTD Data Publications Guide.  Agencies that receive Chapter 53 funds and own, operate, or manage capital assets in public transportation are also required to file an annual report, even if they do not receive §5307 or §5311 funds. Agencies that do not receive or benefit from FTA funding may elect to submit their data to the NTD as Voluntary Reporter but are still assigned a reporter type.

columns.name: Mode
columns.description: A system for carrying transit passengers described by specific right-of-way (ROW), technology, and operational features

columns.name: State Name
columns.description: The name of the state in which the served place/county combination is located. Note that a place may lie in two states, but a county does not. Therefore, a place/county combination is unique to a given state.

description: This dataset details places and counties served by Demand Response (DR) modes for each applicable agency and type of service (TOS) reported to the National Transit Database for Report Year 2023. NTD Data Tables organize and summarize data from the 2023 National Transit Database in a manner that is more useful for quick reference and summary analysis. name: Truck Size and Weight Enforcement Data
description: This dataset consists of truck size and weight enforcement data including number of trucks weighed, number of violations, and number of oversize/overweight permits, as reported by the States in their annual certification to FHWA.

name: 2022 - 2024 NTD Annual Data - Metrics

columns.name: Total Operating Expenses Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Passengers per Hour Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Cost per Hour
columns.description: The annual average operating expense incurred to operate one vehicle/passenger car for one hour of passenger service.

columns.name: Cost per Passenger Mile Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Fare Revenues per Total Operating Expense (Recovery Ratio) Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: TOS
columns.description: Describes how public transportation services are provided by the transit agency: directly operated (DO) or purchased transportation (PT) services.

columns.name: Fare Revenues per Unlinked Passenger Trip Questionable
columns.description: FTA marks a data point as Questionable when there is reason to believe it is incorrect, but the reporting agency has been unable to correct the data or offer an explanation for its anomalous appearance.

columns.name: Primary UZA Name
columns.description: The name of the agency's Urbanized Area.

columns.name: Primary UZA UACE Code
columns.description: UACE Code remains consistent across census years.

columns.name: Cost per Passenger
columns.description: The annual average operating expense incurred to transport one passenger from the beginning of her trip to the end.

columns.name: Organization Type
columns.description: Description of the agency's legal entity.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: A five-digit identifying number for each agency used in the current NTD system.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: The transit agency's name.

columns.name: Passenger Miles
columns.description: The sum of the distances ridden by all passengers during the entire Fiscal Year.

columns.name: Vehicle Revenue Hours
columns.description: Total number of hours that vehicles/passenger cars traveled while in revenue service during the report year. Includes both typical and atypical service. Excludes deadhead.

columns.name: Unlinked Passenger Trips
columns.description: The number of passengers who boarded public transportation vehicles. Passengers are counted each time they board a vehicle no matter how many vehicles they use to travel from their origin to their destination.

columns.name: Cost per Passenger Questionable

columns.name: lanes
columns.description: Stringified array of JSON objects identifying and describing individual lanes within a road event. Each lane JSON object can contain the following fields: “order” - The position of a lane in sequence on the roadway. This value is used as an index to indicate the order of all WZDx lanes provided for a road event; “type” - an indication of the type of lane or shoulder.); “status” - status of the lane for the traveling public; “lane_number” - the number assigned to the lane to help identify its position. Flexible, but usually used for regular, driveable lanes; “restrictions” - an array of lane_restriction JSON objects. Each lane_restriction JSON object contain the following fields: “restriction_type” - the type of restriction being enforced; “restriction_value” - the measure of the restriction type; “restriction_units” - units of measure for the restriction value. The “restriction_type” field of the lane_restriction JSON object is required. The “order”, “type”, and “status” fields of the lane JSON object are required.

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_contact_email
columns.description: The email address of the individual or group responsible for the data feed.

columns.name: beginning_milepost
columns.description: The linear distance measured against a milepost marker along a roadway where the event begins.

columns.name: stroke
columns.description: The color of the GeoJSON stroke.

columns.name: relationship_next

columns.name: stroke-opacity
columns.description: The opacity of the GeoJSON stroke.

columns.name: direction
columns.description: The digitization direction of the road that is impacted by the event. This value is based on the standard naming for US roadways and indicates the direction the traffic flow regardless of the real heading angle.

columns.name: types_of_work
columns.description: Stringified array of JSON objects indicating the type of work being done in a road event, if applicable (e.g. typical work zones), as well as noting if the type of work will result in an architectural change to the roadway. Each JSON object can contain the following fields: “type_of_work_id” - ID identifying the types_of_work record; “type_name” - a high-level text description of the type of work being done; “is_architectural_change” - an optional boolean flag indicating whether the type of work will result in an architectural change to the roadway.

columns.name: road_number

columns.name: relationship_parents

columns.name: data_source_organization_name

columns.name: update_date

columns.name: end_date

columns.name: data_source_lrs_type

columns.name: creation_date

columns.name: event_type

columns.name: data_source_id

columns.name: road_event_id

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_update_date

columns.name: road_event_feed_info_update_frequency

columns.name: data_source_location_method
columns.description: The typical method used to locate the beginning and end of a work zone impact area.

description: The WZDx Specification enables infrastructure owners and operators (IOOs) to make harmonized work zone data available for third party use. The intent is to make travel on public roads safer and more efficient through ubiquitous access to data on work zone activity. Specifically, the project aims to get data on work zones into vehicles to help automated driving systems (ADS) and human drivers navigate more safely. MCDOT leads the effort to aggregate and collect work zone data from the AZTech Regi

columns.name: Simple At-Grade Platform Station
columns.description: Number of stations on-street or in street or highway medians. May be low-level platforms (serving low-floor vehicles) or raised platforms (serving high-floor vehicles). Typically includes shelters, canopies, lighting, signage, and/or ticket vending machines.

columns.description: Number of stand-alone buildings or structures inventoried by each agency equipment for cleaning underfloor equipment of rail rolling stock.

columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency wherein where revenue collection personnel process electronic and/or cash fare payments.

columns.name: Heavy Maintenance & Overhaul (Backshop)
columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency wherein mechanics, machinists and other maintenance personnel perform heavy overhaul and other related rebuilding activities to help revenue vehicles reach their targeted service life.

columns.name: Bus Transfer Center
columns.description: Number of inventoried terminal stations for several routes or a large mid-route transfer facility where passengers may connect between two or more fixed-route bus services.

columns.name: At-Grade Fixed Guideway Station
columns.description: Number of inventoried passenger stations located at street grade along a transit exclusive right-of-way. May include pedestrian overpasses to allow passengers to reach station.

columns.name: Vehicle Fueling Facility
columns.description: Number of stand-alone buildings or structures inventoried by each agency containing vehicle fuel dispensing equipment.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: UZA Name
columns.description: The name of the agency's Urbanized Area.

columns.name: Agency VOMS
columns.description: The number of revenue vehicles operated across the whole agency to meet the annual maximum service requirement. This is the revenue vehicle count during the peak season of the year; on the week and day that maximum service is provided. Vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) exclude atypical days and one-time special events.

columns.name: General Purpose Maintenance Facility/Depot
columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency wherein mechanics and other maintenance department personnel, provide basic service readiness inspection (e.g. tire pressure, oil/fluid levels) and light repair (e.g. mirror replacement) or service (e.g. sweeping) on revenue.

columns.name: Administrative Office / Sales Office
columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency which house the executive management and supporting activities for transit operations, with the exception of vehicle maintenance, that could include accounting, finance, engineering, legal, safety, security, customer services, scheduling and planning. These buildings may include customer information or ticket sale offices, which are owned by the transit agency but not part of passenger stations.

columns.name: Combined Administrative and Maintenance Facility
columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency wherein facility with combined functions of at least one of the administrative facilities and one of the maintenance facilities.

columns.name: Other, Administrative & Maintenance
columns.description: Number of inventoried administrative or maintenance facilities that do not fit into one of the ten categories described.

columns.name: Elevated Fixed Guideway Station
columns.description: Number of inventoried passenger stations located above grade built on a viaduct, a steel or concrete structure, or on retained fill.

columns.name: Underground Fixed Guideway Station
columns.description: Number of passenger stations inventoried by each agency which typically consist of a concrete structure built below grade, constructed by cut and cover, drill-and-blast, excavated, bored tunnel, or sunken underwater tube.

columns.name: Exclusive Grade-Separated Platform Station
columns.description: Number of stations along the street or in street or highway medians that are separated from mixed traffic. May be low-level platforms (serving low-floor vehicles) or raised platforms (serving high-floor vehicles).

columns.name: Surface Parking Lot
columns.description: Number of lots inventoried by each agency which are paved with asphalt, concrete, or permeable materials with parking spaces outlined by paint and other materials for demarcation. Typically includes lanes for vehicle circulation and is usually uncovered.

columns.name: Parking Structure
columns.description: Number of inventoried single or multi-level parking structures inventoried by each agency that are built either underground (typically underneath a building or station), above grade, or both. Characterized by a street-level entrance with ramps to access parking spaces below the surface.

columns.name: UACE Code

columns.name: Maintenance Facility (Service and Inspection)
columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency wherein mechanics, machinists and other maintenance personnel perform preventive maintenance, daily service and inspection, and/or corrective maintenance activities on revenue vehicles to keep them in-service.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: A five-digit identifying number for each agency used in the current NTD system.

columns.name: Total Facilities

columns.name: Administrative and Other Non-Passenger Facilities

columns.name: Other, Passenger or Parking

columns.name: Vehicle Testing Facility
columns.description: Number of facilities inventoried by each agency which are used for vehicle acceptance testing (after being received from manufacturer or overhauls or other maintenance activity).

columns.name: Vehicle Washing Facility
columns.description: Number of stand-alone buildings or structures inventoried by each agency containing vehicle washer equipment.

columns.name: Ferryboat Terminal
columns.description: Number of terminals inventoried by each agency wherein passengers may board or alight from the ferryboat. Terminals may include canopies or shelters, lighting, and signage.

description: This dataset details station/facility types and counts for each applicable agency reported to the National Transit Database in the 2022, 2023, and 2024 report years. NTD Data Tables organize and summarize data from the National Transit Database in a manner that is more useful for quick reference and summary analysis. This dataset is based on the 2022 - 2024 Transit Facilities and Transit Stations database files. In years 2015-2021, you can find this data in the "Facilities and Stations" data tab

name: Advanced Messaging Concept Development: Probe Vehicle Data
provider: U.S. Department of Transportation’s (USDOT) Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) Joint Program Office (JPO) -- Recommended citation: "U.S. Department of Transportation Intelligent Transportation Systems Joint Program Office. (2016). Advanced Messaging Concept Development: Probe Vehicle Data. [Dataset]. Provided by ITS DataHub through Data.transportation.gov. Accessed YYYY-MM-DD from http://doi.org/10.21949/1504475"

columns.name: Elevation
columns.description: The elevation of the vehicle in meters above mean sea level as determined by the OBU GPS when the PVD was sent.

columns.name: Speed
columns.description: The vehicle speed in meters per second.

columns.name: Time Message Container Created
columns.description: The time at which the PVD message container was created on the OBU prior to being sent to the VCC Cloud server in milliseconds UTC time. Empty

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: The geographic latitude position as determined by the OBU GPS unit when the PVD was sent, expressed as a GPS coordinate.

columns.name: Heading
columns.description: The heading of the vehicle expressed in degrees (0-360) where zero represents “North” and positive values express a heading values in a clockwise direction from “North.”

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: The geographic longitude position as determined by the OBU GPS unit when the PVD was sent expressed as a GPS coordinate.

columns.name: Mode of Transmission
columns.description:
An indication of which mode of transmission was used to transmit the message (DSRC or Cellular) from the OBU to the VCC Cloud server.
If received via DSRC:
	1 through 114 = The ID of the RSU used to forward the message to the server 
If received via cellular: 
	999999 = Cellular

columns.name: Count
columns.description: The total number of snapshots contained in the parent PVD message.

columns.name: Snapshot Time
columns.description: The time at which the snapshot was captured expressed as milliseconds UTC time.

columns.name: Snapshot Count Number
columns.description: The snapshot sequence number within the parent PVD message of the data represented in the current row of the CSV data file.

columns.name: Time Received
columns.description: The time at which the PVD was received by the VCC Cloud server in milliseconds UTC time.

columns.name: OBU ID
columns.description: The ID of the OBU that generated and sent the message for the current communication sequence.

columns.name: PVD Message ID
columns.description: A message ID value generated by the OBU that increases sequentially with each new PVD message created starting with 1. Empty

columns.name: Snapshot Wiper Status
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s front windshield wipers expressed as:
	Blank = Unavailable
	‘off’ = Off
	‘intermittent’  = Intermittent

columns.name: Snapshot ABS Active
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s ABS where the system was detected to be active. 
	0 = Unavailable
	1 = Off
	2 = On
	3 = Engaged

columns.name: Snapshot Precipitation Sensor
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s on board sensors that detect the presence of precipitation expressed as:
	0 = None
	1 = Light Mist
	2 = Heavy Mist
	3 = Light Rain
	4 = Rain
	5 = Moderate Rain
	6 = Heavy Rain
	7 = Heavy Downpour

columns.name: Test No.
columns.description: The AMCD test number the record was collected during.

columns.name: Snapshot Stability Control Status
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s stability control system. If the vehicle is equipped with an SCS, the element reports whether the system was in an Off or On state.
	0 = Unavailable
	1 = Off
	2 = On

columns.name: Snapshot Speed
columns.description: The vehicle’s speed in meters per second at the time the snapshot was captured.

columns.name: Snapshot Elevation
columns.description: The elevation of the vehicle in meters above mean sea level as determined by the OBU GPS when the snapshot was captured.

columns.name: Snapshot Traction Control State
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s traction system. If the vehicle is equipped with a TCS, the element reports whether the system was in an Off, On or Engaged state.
	0 = Unavailable
	1 = Off
	2 = On
	3 = Engaged

columns.name: Snapshot Ambient Air Temperature
columns.description: The temperature of the air outside the vehicle as measured by vehicle sensors and expressed as degrees Celsius.

columns.name: Snapshot Steering Wheel Angle
columns.description: The angle of the driver’s steering wheel, expressed in degrees of rotation from center where positive values represent rotation in the clockwise direction and negative values represent rotation in the counterclockwise direction.

columns.name: Snapshot Vertical Acceleration
columns.description: A data element representing the signed vertical acceleration of the vehicle along the vertical axis in meters per second squared where positive values indicate upward (opposite of gravity) acceleration.

columns.name: Snapshot Lateral Acceleration
columns.description: The acceleration along the Y axis or perpendicular to the vehicle's general direction of travel in parallel with a left-to right centerline. Expressed in meters per second squared where negative values indicate the vehicle is accelerating to left.

columns.name: Snapshot Longitudinal Acceleration
columns.description: The acceleration along the X axis or the vehicle's direction of travel, which is generally in parallel with a front to rear centerline. Expressed in meters per second squared where negative values indicate deceleration, and possible braking action.

columns.name: Snapshot Ambient Air Pressure
columns.description: The pressure of the air outside the vehicle as measured by vehicle sensors and expressed as hPa.

columns.name: Snapshot Yaw Rate
columns.description: A signed value representing degrees rotation per second of the vehicle around its vertical axis. Positive values indicate clockwise rotation of the vehicle around its vertical axis.

columns.name: Snapshot Longitude
columns.description: The geographic longitude position as determined by the OBU GPS unit when the snapshot was captured expressed as a GPS coordinate.

columns.name: Snapshot Tire Air Pressure
columns.description: The status of the vehicle’s combined tire pressure status. A measure of the relative tire pressure observed. Encoded as per the value set used in SAE J1939.

columns.name: Snapshot Latitude
columns.description: The geographic latitude position as determined by the OBU GPS unit when the snapshot was captured, expressed as a GPS coordinate.

columns.name: Snapshot Heading
columns.description: The heading of the vehicle at the time the snapshot was captured expressed in degrees (0-360) where zero represents “North” and positive values express a heading values in a clockwise direction from “North.”

columns.name: Snapshot Brake Applied Status
columns.description:
A bit field representing a binary indication of whether brakes were being applied at each wheel. A component of the brake system status data frame.
	0000 = All off
	0001 = Left front active
	0010 = Left rear active
	0100 = Right front active
	1000 = Right rear active
	1111 = Brake active

columns.name: Snapshot Light Status
columns.description:
The status of the vehicle’s external lighting system expressed as a bit field where:
	Bit0 = Off
	Bit1 = Headlight Low Beam
	Bit2 = Headlight High Beam
	Bit3 = Left Turn 
	Bit4 = Right Turn
	Bit5 = Hazard
	Bit6 = Automatic Control
	Bit7 = Daytime Running

tags: category: traveler information and personal mobility
description: Contains all PVDs generated during the AMCD field testing program. The probe vehicle message is used to exchange status about a vehicle with other DSRC readers to allow the collection of information about a typical vehicle’s traveling behaviors along a segment of road. The exchanges of this message as well as the event which caused the collection of various elements defined in the messages are in Annex B of the SAE J2735 standard.

name: Revocation - All With History
description: *Dataset* Information on carrier/broker/freight forwarder authorities that have been revoked by FMCSA. The dataset includes the DOT number and docket number of the entity, the type of authority revoked, and the reason. See dataset attachment "FMCSA Dataset Description and Data Definitions - Select Datasets" for more information.

name: BOC3 - All With History
description: *Dataset* Records for each BOC3 agent hired by a carrier/broker/freight forwarder. Each entity must hire a BOC3 agent to represent them in legal matters to obtain operating authority. In some cases, entities may act as their own BOC3 agent. The records in the dataset contain the BOC3 agent’s name and address. The dataset also contains the DOT number and docket number of the represented entity. See dataset attachment "FMCSA Dataset Description and Data Definitions - Select Datasets" for more info

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_maneuverAssistList
columns.description: Assist data consisting of a list of ConnectionManeuverAssist entries.  This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_timeStamp
columns.description: Timestamp of the Signal Phase and Timing (SPAT) message, expressed in minute of the year - the number of elapsed minutes of the current year in the time system being used (typically UTC time). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_externalID
columns.description: External ID

columns.name: metadata_psid
columns.description: Provider Service Identifier. A number that identifies a service provided by an application. PSID is defined in IEEE Std 1609.12.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_moy
columns.description: Minute of current UTC year, used only with messages to be archived.  This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_id_id
columns.description: SPAT intersection reference ID for a single intersection, consisting of a regionID and intersection ID assignment, and provides a unique mapping to the intersection MAP in question which provides complete location and approach/move/lane data. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_RSUID
columns.description: Identifier of road side unit.

columns.name: metadata_logFileName
columns.description: Name of the original file that deposited the message.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_revision
columns.description: A sequence number within a stream of messages with the same DSRCmsgID and from the same sender. The receipt of a non-sequential MsgCount value (from the same sending device and message type) implies that one or more messages from that sending device may have been lost, unless MsgCount has been re-initialized due to an identity change. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_enabledLanes
columns.description: Enabled lanes list - a list of lanes where the RevocableLane bit has been set which are now active and therefore part of the current intersection. Used for intersection at REL ramp.  This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_generatedAt
columns.description: Closest time to which the record was created, either signed or received by the generatedBy source in UTC format. This information is taken from the communication header.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_name
columns.description: Human readable name for this collection of SPAT messages - to be used only in debug mode. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_states_MovementState
columns.description: Signal phase state mapping to the lanes it applies to, and point in time it will end. It may contain both active and future states. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: dataType
columns.description: The data type.

columns.name: randomNum
columns.description: Random decimal number, to be used for random sampling of data within Socrata. This field is created for use within Socrata and is not present in the data Sandbox.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_status
columns.description: General status of the controller(s). This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_timeStamp
columns.description: The mSec point in the current UTC minute that the message was constructed. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_schemaVersion
columns.description: Version of the metadata schema.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_regional
columns.description: Regional Extension. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_generatedBy
columns.description: Source of the record, whether [OBU, RSU, TMC].

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_regional
columns.description: Regional Extension. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_name
columns.description: Minute of current UTC year, used only with messages to be archived.  This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: payload_data_SPAT_intersections_IntersectionState_id_region
columns.description: Road Regulator ID - a globally unique regional assignment value, typically assigned to a regional DOT authority. The value zero is used for testing. This field is based on the J2735 Standard.

columns.name: metadata_kind
columns.description: Metadata kind.

tags: tampa connected vehicle pilot deployment (tampa cv pilot)
subcategory: connected vehicle traffic signal system
description: The Tampa CV Pilot generates data from the interaction between vehicles and between vehicles and infrastructure. This dataset consists of Signal Phasing and Timing Message (SPaT) Messages transmitted by road-side units (RSU) located throughout the Tampa CV Pilot Study area. The full set of raw, SPaT data from Tampa CV Pilot can be requested from the ITS DataHub Sandbox. The data fields follow SAE J2735 data frames (Section 6) and structure (Section 7). tags: category: traveler information and personal mobility
description: This dataset contains a sample of the broadcast Traveler Information Messages (TIM) being generated by the Wyoming Connected Vehicle (CV) Pilot. This dataset only contains SchemaVersion 6 TIM sample data from December 18, 2018 to present. It is updated hourly and will hold up to 3 million of the most recent TIM records. columns.name: PhaseCheck2
columns.description: This attribute examines the phases 2 and 6 for checking the possible phase errors. Possible values are: 1, indicating the red; 3 indicating green; and 4 indicating yellow.

columns.name: CumulativePhaseCheck2
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current phasecheck2.

columns.name: CumulativePhaseCheck1
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current phasecheck1.

columns.name: CurrentIndication_MinorStreet
columns.description: This attribute defines the current indication for a minor street approach (or barrier 2). Possible values are: 1, indicating the red; 3 indicating green; and 4 indicating yellow.

columns.name: PhaseCheck1
columns.description: This attribute examines the phases 2 and 5 for checking the possible phase errors. Possible values are: 1, indicating the red; 3 indicating green; and 4 indicating yellow.

columns.name: Duration_Indication_MinorStreet
columns.description: This attribute defines the duration of the current indication in second for a minor street approach (or barrier 2).

columns.name: CumulativeTime3
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current data file.

columns.name: CurrentIndication_MajorStreet
columns.description: This attribute defines the current indication for a major street approach (or Barrier 1). Possible values are: 1, indicating the red; 3 indicating green; and 4 indicating yellow.

columns.name: Duration_Indication_MajorStreet
columns.description: This attribute defines the duration of the current indication in second for a major street approach (or barrier 1).

columns.name: CumulativeTime1
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current data file.

columns.name: CumulativeTime2
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current data file.

columns.name: Phase_Ring2
columns.description: This attribute is a unique identifier for the phase of Ring 2.

columns.name: CumulativeCurrentState1
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current state of the phase of Ring 1.

columns.name: Phase_Ring1
columns.description: This attribute is a unique identifier for the phase of Ring 1.

columns.name: Timeremaining_Phase_Ring2
columns.description: This attribute specifies the time remaining (in seconds) before the signal phase will change to the next phase (for yellow and red indications).

columns.name: Timeremaining_Phase_Ring1
columns.description: This attribute specifies the time remaining (in seconds) before the signal phase will change to the next phase (for yellow and red indications).

columns.name: CumulativeCurrentState2
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current state of the phase of Ring 2.

columns.name: Duration_Indication_MinorStreet2
columns.description: This attribute defines the duration of the current indication in second for a minor street approach (or barrier 2). This field is same with Duration_Indication_MinorStreet2.

columns.name: RSE_Number
columns.description: NA

columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: This field contains the time in deci-seconds with an Epoch time format.

columns.name: CumulativeGreenRing1
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current green indication of the phase of Ring 1.

columns.name: CumulativeGreenRing2
columns.description: This attribute shows the cumulative time (in seconds) of the current green indication of the phase of Ring 2.

tags: subcategory: connected vehicle traffic signal system
description: Data were collected during the Multi-Modal Intelligent Transportation Signal Systems (MMITSS) study. MMITSS is a next-generation traffic signal system that seeks to provide a comprehensive traffic information framework to service all modes of transportation.The Signal Plans for Roadside Equipment (RSE) data contains the basics of a Signal Phase and Timing (SPAT) message. This data includes SPAT message and the timestamp of the SPAT message. The data also provides the signal phase and timing info

name: ActPendInsur - All With History
description: *Dataset* Information on the implementation dates of an active or pending insurance policy (posted date, effective date and cancel effective date). In addition to these dates, the record contains the insurance company name, the BI&PD underlying limit and maximum limit amounts, and the DOT number and docket number of the carrier/broker/freight forwarder that holds the policy. See dataset attachment "FMCSA Dataset Description and Data Definitions - Select Datasets" for more information.

name: AuthHist - All With History
description: *Dataset* Records showing the history of each authority granted to a carrier/broker/freight forwarder, along with the dates of the original authority action (e.g., “granted”) and the final authority action (e.g., “revoked”). The dataset contains the DOT number and docket number of the entity that holds or held the authority. As there can be multiple authorities for a single entity, there may be multiple records for an entity. See dataset attachment "FMCSA Dataset Description and Data Definitions

columns.name: asdBundles_asdRecords_broadcastInst_biTxChannel
columns.description: This field contains the transmission channel of the broadcast message. Possible values are chControl, chService, and ch172, 174,176, 178, 780, 182, and 184.

columns.name: asdBundles_asdRecords_broadcastInst_biTxMode
columns.description: This field contains the transmission mode of the broadcast message. For the TSD dataset, this data element takes only one value: {alternating}

columns.name: asdBundles_asdRecords_broadcastInst_biPriority
columns.description: This field contains the priority of the broadcast message, presented in an 8 bit binary string. The 5 least significant bits are reserved (and set to 0). A priority of all zeros is used for routing messages such as roadside signage where not displaying the message to the driver is only of modest impact. A value of 111xxxxx indicates a message of the highest priority. Other priorities are set according to local conventions.  Values in decimal should be converted back to the 8 bit binary string and interpreted as described above.  For example, a value of 32 is 00100000 in binary and corresponds to a broadcast priority of 1 (low).

columns.name: asdBundles_asdRecords_broadcastInst_biEncryption

columns.name: asdBundles_asdRecords_advisoryMessage
columns.description: This field contains a complete TIM according to the SAE J2735 Standard which is based on a tag-length-value structure of the ASN.1 BER-DER formatted data. To provide insight into decoding these entries the following table will present the breakdown of a single message and summarize each of its components.  As one will see in the table below, critical to decoding these entries in the translation of the ITIS codes into their meaning.  To translate these codes user are advised to consult the SAE J2540 Standard.  TIM messages, which are contained in this *_advisoryMessage field is comprised of three parts: Part I is header, Part II is common anchor, Part III is content, all of them are colored coded in the table below.  Additionally, in the table below data element are shown in two colors, blue is for data type and red is data name.

columns.name: timeToLive
columns.description: This field contains the time remaining before the ISD message expires.  This field is enumerated as follows: 0 – 1 minute, 1 – 30 minutes, 2- one day, 3 – one week, 4 – one month, 5 – one year

columns.name: serviceRegion_nwCorner_lat
columns.description: This field contains the latitude of the northwest corner of the rectangle that the SDW presides over, expressed in 1/10th integer microdegrees. The value 900000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: serviceRegion_seCorner_lat
columns.description: This field contains the latitude of the southeast corner of the rectangle that the SDW presides over, expressed in 1/10th integer microdegrees. The value 900000001 shall be used when unavailable

columns.name: map_int_approach_drivingLanes_nodeList
columns.description: This field contains the sequence of signed offset values for determining the Xs and Ys (and, possibly Width or Zs when present) using the current intersection position to build a path for the enclosing reference lane relating to a lane in the current intersection.  Please consult page 77 of the SAE J2735 Standard for more information.

columns.name: map_int_approach_name
columns.description: This field contains an optional human readable name for the approach lanes to the intersection.

columns.name: map_layerType
columns.description: This field specifies the type of information found in a layer of a geographic map fragment (such as an intersection). For the ISD dataset, this data element takes only one value: “intersectionData”

columns.name: map_int_approaches_approach_drivingLanes_
columns.description: This field contains the width of a lane in in centimeters. The maximum value would be a lane of over 327 centimeters.

columns.name: map_int__approach_drivingLanes_laneNumber
columns.description: This field contains a unique index value for a lane used to refer to that lane by other objects in the intersection map data structure. Lanes may be ingress (inbound traffic) or egress (outbound traffic) in nature, as well as barriers and other types of specialty lanes. All lanes are numbered. The laneNumber, in conjunction with the intersections_id, forms a regionally unique way to address a specific lane in that intersection.

columns.name: mapData_int_refPoint_long
columns.description: This field contains the longitude of a reference point at the described intersection, expressed in 1/10th integer microdegrees.  The value 1800000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: map_int_refPoint_lat
columns.description: This field contains the latitude of a reference point at the described intersection, expressed in 1/10th integer microdegrees.  The value 900000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: map_int_id
columns.description: This field contains an ID (number) that globally and uniquely defines an intersection within a country or region. Assignment rules for this value are established elsewhere and may use regional assignment schemas that vary.

columns.name: map_int_name
columns.description: This field contains an optional human readable name for the intersection.

columns.name: dialogID
columns.description: This field contains the ID of the type of data being transmitted.  In this instance all entries will be “intersectionSitDataDep” indicating that intersection situation data (ISD) is being transmitted / presented here.

columns.name: spat_timestamp_minute
columns.description: Minute when the SPaT message was generated in GMT.

columns.name: spat_timestamp_hour
columns.description: Hour when the SpaT message was generated in GMT.

columns.name: spat_timestamp_year
columns.description: Year when the SPaT message was generated in GMT.

columns.name: map_crc
columns.description: This attribute is a two byte data element calculated over the payload bytes of the message (starting with the initial sequence and ending with the last data element before the CRC itself and including all tag, length, and values bytes found in between). It is always placed as the very last data element in the message. The generating polynomial used is the "CRC-CCITT" commonly expressed as x16 + x12 + x5 + 1. An initial seed value of zero shall be used. Note that because the first byte of every DSRC message is never zero (it is 0x30), framing errors due to incorrectly clocking initial zero values cannot occur. Note that the MSB byte is always transmitted first, following the typical ASN bytes order. When a well formed DSRC message (including its last two bytes holding the CRC value) is decoded and input to the CRC process, the resulting CRC should always be the value zero.

columns.name: spat_timestamp_second
columns.description: Second when the SPaT message was generated in GMT.

columns.name: spat_int_states_yellStateConfidence
columns.description: This field describes the confidence of the yellow signal phase and its estimated time values.  Values are enumerated as follows: 0 – unknownEstimate, 1 – minTime, 2- maxTime, 3 - timeLikelyToChange

columns.name: spat_int_states_stateConfidence
columns.description: This field describes the confidence of the current signal phase and its estimated time values.  Values are enumerated as follows: 0 – unknownEstimate, 1 – minTime, 2- maxTime, 3 - timeLikelyToChange

columns.name: spat_int_states_currState
columns.description: This attribute defines the current state of a particular known movement and depends on the type of lane that the currState applies to.

columns.name: spat_int_states_timeToChange
columns.description: This attribute specifies the time remaining, in tenths of a second, before the signal phase will change to the next phase.  The maximum time remaining is 12000 (120.0 seconds = 2 minutes).  0 indicates no time remaining.  A special value of 12001 indicates indefinite time remaining (greater than 2 minutes) and a special value of 12002 indicates an unknown amount of time remaining.

columns.name: map_int_approach_drivingLanes_laneAttributes
columns.description: The laneAttributes data element relates the allowed (possible) movements from a motorized vehicle lane. Note that in practice these values may be further restricted by vehicle class, local regulatory environment and other changing conditions.  There are 17 enumerated values for the Lane Attributes data element.

columns.name: spat_int_states_laneSet
columns.description: This attribute represents movement within lanes at an intersection.  Data is provided in hexadecimal.  Each movement/lane pair is provided as a double-octet (four character) hex string.  The first two characters represent the movement, while the last two characters represent the lane.  The movement is obtained by converting the hexadecimal value to an 8 bit binary string with bits 76543210.  A movement is present with a logical 1 for any given bit.  Bit 0 represents straight motion, bit 1 represents a left turn, bit 2 represents a right turn, and bit 3 represents a U-turn.  No other bits are used.  For example, a laneSet string of 0x0703 has the movement characters 07, which are written as 00000111 in binary.  Bits 0, 1, and 2 are in the logical 1 position, indicating straight, left, and right movement.  The string has lane characters 03 which directly indicate lane 3.  Therefore, a laneSet of 0x0703 indicates straight/left/right movement on lane 3.  There is no limit to the number of movement lane/pairs possible.  For example, a laneSet of 0x08040302 indicates a U-Turn on lane 4 (with movement string 00001000 in binary) and straight/left on lane 2 (with movement string 00000011 in binary). Values in decimal should be converted back to the full binary bit string in order to interpret the laneSet field.

columns.name: spat_int_status
columns.description: This attribute contains the Advance Traffic Controller status information on the intersection.  Data is encoded in hexadecimal.  Converting the hexadecimal to binary gives an 8 bit unsigned integer with bits 76543210.  Each bit has value 0 or 1 and indicates whether or not a condition is present at an intersection.  For the ISD dataset, this data element takes only one value: {0}  which indicates the intersection is operating normally.

columns.name: map_msgCnt
columns.description: This field contains a sequence number within a stream of messages with the same msgID and from the same sender. A sender may initialize this element to any value in the range 0-127 when sending the first message with a given msgID, or if the sender has changed identity since sending the most recent message with that msgID. Two further use cases exist when the sender has not changed identity: When the rest of the message content to be sent changes, the msgCnt shall be set equal to one greater than the value used in the most recent message sent with the same msgID. When the message content has not changed, the msgCnt is not changed. For this element the value after 127 is zero. The receipt of a non-sequential msgCnt value (from the same sending device and message type) implies that one or more messages from that sending device may have been lost, unless msgCnt has been reinitialized due to an identity change

columns.name: serviceRegion_nwCorner_long

columns.description: This field contains the bundle number of the ISD message

columns.name: spat_timestamp_day
columns.description: Day when the SPaT message was generated in GMT.

columns.name: spat_int_id
columns.description: The unique ID number for the SPaT message.

columns.name: seqID
columns.description: This field communicate the message content type.  All entries in this instance will be “data” indicating that the data portion of the transmitted message is presented.

columns.name: map_int_approach_id
columns.description: This field contains a unique index value for an approach or egress in an intersection for the convenience of human users. It is typically used along with an optional human readable string name for the object.

columns.name: map_msgID
columns.description: The msgID is a data element used in each message to define which type of message follows from the message set defined by this Standard. This data element is always the first value inside the message and is used to tell the receiving application how to interpret the remaining bytes (i.e. what message structure has been used). For the ISD dataset, this data element takes only one value: “mapData”

columns.name: schema
columns.description: This field contains the ID (number) of the schema that was used to convert the data from binary to human readable (text) format.  The values will either be 20, indicating that the ASN.1.2.0 schema was used in the conversion, or 21, indicating that the ASN.1.2.1 was used in the conversion process.

columns.name: type
columns.description: This field contains the type of VSD message.  When converted to decimal, message types are enumerated as follows: 1- VSD message contains only the fundamental data elements, 2 – the VSD message contains the VehicleStatus data frame, 4 – the VSD message contains weather data, 8 – the VSD message contains environmental data, and 16 – the VSD message contains electric vehicle data

columns.name: bundle_tempID
columns.description: This field contains the ID (number) for the bundle.

columns.name: bundle_pos_elevation_long
columns.description: The Elevation data element represents the geographic position above or below the reference ellipsoid (typically WSG-84), in decimeter. This data element initially recorded as a 16-bit (hexadecimal) number has a resolution of 1 decimeter and represents an asymmetric range of positive and negative values. The encoding is as follows: the range 0x0000 to 0xEFFF (0 to 61439 decimal) are positive numbers representing elevations from 0 to +6143.9 meters, i.e. above the reference ellipsoid. The range 0xF001 to 0xFFFF are negative numbers representing elevations from -409.5 meters to -0.1 meters, i.e. below the reference ellipsoid. An elevation higher than +6143.9 meters is represented 0xEFFF. An elevation lower than -409.5 meters is represented 0xF001. If the sending device does not know its elevation it shall encode the Elevation data element with 0xF000. Examples of this encoding are: the elevation 0 meters is encoded as 0x0000. The elevation -0.1 meters is encoded as 0xFFFF. The elevation +100.0 meters is encoded as 0x03E8.

columns.name: bundle_time_minute
columns.description: This field contains the minute in which the bundle was generated.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_accelSet
columns.description: This field contains encoded information about the vehicle’s acceleration.  It contains the longitudinal, lateral, and vertical acceleration, as well as the yaw rate.  Please consult page 44 of the SAE J2735 Standard for further information.  Note the decoded records from this field are provided in many of the subsequent columns of data that follow this field.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_speed_metersPerSecond
columns.description: This field contains the speed, in meter per second, of the vehicle referenced by the bundle.

columns.name: bundle_time_hour
columns.description: This field contains the hour in which the bundle was generated.

columns.name: bundle_time_day
columns.description: This field contains the day in which the bundle was generated.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_speed_Transmission
columns.description: This field contains the transmission state of the vehicle. When converted to decimal, transmission states are enumerated as follows: 0 – neutral, speed relative to the vehicle alignment, 1 – park, speed relative to the vehicle alignment, 2 – forward gears, speed relative to the vehicle alignment, 3 – reverse gears, speed relative to the vehicle alignment, 4-6 reserved, 7 – unavailable (vehicle not equipped) and E undefined.

columns.name: crc
columns.description: A two byte data element calculated over the payload bytes of the message (starting with the initial sequence and ending with the last data element before the CRC itself and including all tag, length, and values bytes found in between). Always placed as the very last data element in the message. The generating polynomial used is the "CRC-CCITT" commonly expressed as x^16 + x^12 + x^5 + 1. An initial seed value of zero shall be used. Note that because the first byte of every DSRC message is never zero (it is 0x30), framing errors due to incorrectly clocking initial zero values cannot occur. Note that the MSB byte is always transmitted first, following the typical ASN bytes order. When a well formed DSRC message (including its last two bytes holding the CRC value) is decoded and input to the CRC process, the resulting CRC should always be the value zero.

columns.name: bundle_pos_longitude
columns.description: The geographic longitude of the intersection with reference to the horizontal datum then in use, in degrees. The value 180.0000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_heading_deg
columns.description: The current heading of the front of the vehicle, in degrees from North. North shall be defined as the axis defined by the WSG-84 coordinate system and its reference ellipsoid. Headings "to the east" are defined as the positive direction. A value of 360 is used when unavailable.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_steeringAngle_long
columns.description:
This field contains a value to be converted to degrees to communicate the steering wheel angle.  The LSBs are in units of 1.5 degrees and entries in this field have a range of -126 to +127, which facilitates steering angles between -/+189 degrees and a value signifying that the steering angle is unavailable.  For example:
0 = +1.5 degrees
-126 = -189 degrees and beyond
+126 = +189 degrees and beyond
+127 = unavailable steering angle
More generally, for values between 0 and 126 the steering angle is obtained by multiplying by 1.5 degrees.  However, for values between 129 and 255, mask off the highest bit (which is being used as a sign bit) by doing a bitwise AND with a value of 127. Then swap the remaining bit values by doing a bitwise exclusive OR with a value of 127 and then multiply by -1.5.

columns.name: bundle_time_month
columns.description: This field contains the month in which the bundle was generated.

columns.name: bundle_time_year
columns.description: This field contains the year in which the bundle was generated.

columns.name: path_crumbData_pathHistoryPointSets_04
columns.description: This field contains encoded latitude, longitude, elevation, and time data for a vehicle’s position.  Data is encoded in an octet string. Please consult page 80 of the SAE J2735 Standard for further information.  Note the decoded records from this field are provided in many of the subsequent columns of data that follow this field.

columns.name: path_itemCnt
columns.description: This field contains the number of records in the bundle.  Up to 10 records may be present.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_vehSize_length
columns.description: This field contains the length of the vehicle measured from the edge of the front bumper to the edge of rear bumper expressed in centimeters. 0 is used when the width is unavailable.

columns.name: bundle_time_seconds
columns.description: This field contains the second in which the bundle was generated.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_vehSize_width
columns.description: This field contains the width of the vehicle, in centimeters.  The width is the widest point of the vehicle with all factory installed equipment.  0 is used when the width is unavailable.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_brakes_long
columns.description: To determine the meaning of the entries in this field, the entries have to first be converted into an 8-bitstring (00000000). This string will then be partitioned in accordance with the J2735 Standard to communicate the relevant information regarding the state of a vehicle’s brake system.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_accel_Yaw
columns.description: This field contains the yaw rate of the vehicle, a signed value (to the right being positive) and expressed in 0.01 degrees per second. The yaw rate reports the vehicle's rotation in degrees per second.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_accel_Vert
columns.description: This field contains the signed vertical acceleration of the vehicle along the vertical axis in units of 0.02 G (where 9.80665 meters per second squared is one G, i.e 0.02 G = 0.1962 meters per second squared). This provides an uneven range of over +1.5 to -3.4G in each direction with some negative values for greater negative ranges as denoted. Of note, least significant bit (LSB) units of 0.02 G steps over a range +1.54 to -3.4G and offset by 50 where Value 50 = 0g and Value 0 = -1G.  As further reference a value of +127 = 1.54G,  -120 = -3.4G, -121 for ranges -3.4 to -4.4G, -122 for ranges -4.4 to -5.4G, -123 for ranges -5.4 to -6.4G,    -124 for ranges -6.4 to -7.4G, -125 for ranges -7.4 to -8.4G, -126 for ranges larger than -8.4G, and -127 for unavailable data

columns.name: path_initialPosition_elevation_meters
columns.description: The Elevation data element represents the geographic position above or below the reference ellipsoid (typically WSG-84), in meters. This data element initially recorded as a 16-bit (hexadecimal) number has a resolution of 1 decimeter and represents an asymmetric range of positive and negative values. The encoding is as follows: the range 0x0000 to 0xEFFF (0 to 61439 decimal) are positive numbers representing elevations from 0 to +6143.9 meters, i.e. above the reference ellipsoid. The range 0xF001 to 0xFFFF are negative numbers representing elevations from -409.5 meters to -0.1 meters, i.e. below the reference ellipsoid. An elevation higher than +6143.9 meters is represented 0xEFFF. An elevation lower than -409.5 meters is represented 0xF001. If the sending device does not know its elevation it shall encode the Elevation data element with 0xF000. Examples of this encoding are: the elevation 0 meters is encoded as 0x0000. The elevation -0.1 meters is encoded as 0xFFFF. The elevation +100.0 meters is encoded as 0x03E8.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_accel_Lat
columns.description: This field contains the signed acceleration of the vehicle along the Y axis or perpendicular to the vehicle's direction of travel in parallel with a left-to-right centerline, in units of 0.01 meters per second squared. Negative values indicate left turning action and positive values indicate right-turning action.  A range of over 2Gs is supported.

columns.name: path_initialPosition_latitude
columns.description: The geographic latitude of the vehicle with reference to the horizontal datum then in use, in degrees. The value 90.00000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: bundle_pos_latitude
columns.description: The geographic latitude of the intersection with reference to the horizontal datum then in use, in degrees. The value 90.0000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: path_initialPosition_longitude
columns.description: The geographic longitude of the vehicle with reference to the horizontal datum then in use, in degrees. The value 180.0000001 shall be used when unavailable.

columns.name: bundle_fundamental_accel_Long
columns.description: This field contains the signed acceleration of the vehicle along the X axis or the vehicle's direction of travel in parallel with a front to rear centerline, in units of 0.01 meters per second squared. Negative values indicate braking action.  A range of over 2Gs is supported.

During the ITS World Congress, VSDs were encoded with one of two schemas. The dataset

name: Weekly Transit Service Reference Data

columns.name: End Date
columns.description: The last day of the reference week over which data is collected.

columns.name: Value
columns.description: The value reported for the given Data Point.

columns.name: Reported By
columns.description: Contains a value when the value is reported by a State Department of Transportation on behalf of a subrecipient.

columns.name: Mode
columns.description: Indicates the means of conveyance used for the service. The modes in this list do not reflect all modes reporting to the NTD, rather they are a pre-determined sample of modes which are required by FTA to report weekly reference data.

columns.name: Data Point
columns.description: The data point associated with the given value.  Weekday Unlinked Passenger Trips: The number of boardings on transit vehicles for the given transit agency, mode, and type of service for weekdays occuring between the Begin and End Date (generally 5 weekdays).   Weekday Vehicle Revenue Miles: The number of miles traveled by transit vehicles in revenue service for the given transit agency, mode, and type of service for weekdays occuring between the Begin and End Date (generally 5 weekdays). For Rail Vehicles, this equates to Passenger Car Revenue Miles.   Weekend Unlinked Passenger Trips: The number of boardings on transit vehicles for the given transit agency, mode, and type of service for weekends occuring between the Begin and End Date (generally 5 weekdays).  Weekday Vehicle Revenue Miles: The number of miles traveled by transit vehicles in revenue service for the given transit agency, mode, and type of service for weekends occuring between the Begin and End Date (generally 2 weekend days). For Rail Vehicles, this equates to Passenger Car Revenue Miles.  Weekly Vehicles Operated in Max Service: The maximum number of vehicles operated in maximum service (VOMS) or ""peak service"" by the transit agency for the given reference week.

columns.name: UZA Name
