columns.name: Message ID
columns.description: : A message ID value generated by the PDCM configuration client that increases sequentially with each new PDCM message created starting with 1. Empty

columns.name: SendAll
columns.description: An indicator to send data on all conditions.

columns.name: Termination Distance
columns.description: A distance value that provides a distance-to-live type of time-out. It allows users to provide distance to travel in meters until the probe management process ceases and the default condition is applied.

columns.name: Transmit Interval
columns.description: A time value expressed in seconds that defines time between transmissions of PVD messages.

columns.name: Time Received
columns.description: The time at which the message was received by the OBU in milliseconds UTC time.

columns.name: Snapshot Distance S1
columns.description:
A speed value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based in speed and distance. Two distance and speed pairs are included in the message—D1, S1 and D2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed is < S1 then distance to next snapshot is D1
•	If speed is > S2 then distance to next snapshot is D2
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then distance to snapshot is linearly interpolated between D1 and D2
•	If S1 is zero, then the distance to snapshot is always D1
This is speed S1, which is expressed meters per second.

columns.name: Count
columns.description: A number that provides a count of the number of Vehicle Status Request segments that are included in the message.

columns.name: Vehicle Status Device Type Tag
columns.description:
: An enumeration of the possible values that may be requested from VehicleStatusDeviceType data frame to be used as conditional indicators of whether data will be sent in subsequent PVD messages. The vehicle device type tag values include:
		0 = Unknown 
		1 = Exterior Lights
		2 = Wipers
		3 = Brakes Applied
		4 = Stability Control
		5 = Traction Control
		6 = Anti-lock Brakes
		7 = Sun Sensor
		8 = Rain Sensor
		9 = Air Temperature
		10 = Steering
		11 = Vertical Acceleration Threshold (wheel that exceeded)
		12 = Vertical Acceleration (vertical g-force value)
		13 = Longitudinal Acceleration
		14 = Lateral Acceleration
		15 = Lateral Acceleration Confidence
		16 = Acceleration 4-way
		17 = Confidence Set
		18 = Obstacle Distance
		19 = Obstacle Direction
		20 = Yaw Rate
		21 = Yaw Rate Confidence	
		22 = Date and Time
		23 = Full Position (complete set of time, position, speed, heading)
		24 = Position 2D (lat, lon)
		25 = Position 3D (lat, lon, elevation)
		26 = Vehicle (height, mass, type)
		27 = SpeedHeadC
		28 = SpeedC;

columns.name: Mode of Transmission
columns.description: An indication of which mode of transmission was used to transmit the message (DSRC or Cellular).

columns.name: Send on Less Then Value
columns.description: Threshold for a less than conditional.

columns.name: OBU ID
columns.description: The ID of the OBU that received the message for the current communication sequence

columns.name: Time Sent
columns.description: The time at which the message was sent from the VCC Cloud server to the OBU in milliseconds UTC time.

columns.name: Sample End
columns.description: Allows the Probe Management message to apply its settings to a random sample of vehicles (all vehicles within the stated range defined by Sample Start and Sample End). This uses the last single digit of the current PSN to determine if probe management is to be used. If the current PSN falls between Sample Start and Sample End, then the Probe Data Management policy will be applied.

columns.name: Sample Start
columns.description: Allows the Probe Management message to apply its settings to a random sample of vehicles (all vehicles within the stated range defined by Sample Start and Sample End). This uses the last single digit of the current probe segment number (PSN) to determine if probe management is to be used. If the current PSN falls between Sample Start and Sample End, then the Probe Data Management policy will be applied.

columns.name: Heading Slice
columns.description:
An indication of heading ranges for which the PDCM policy should apply. The bit is set to 1 if the heading slice should be included.
	Bit1 = 0 to 22.5 degrees
	Bit2 = 22.5 to 45 degrees
	Bit3 = 45 to 67.5 degrees
	Bit4 = 67.5 to 90 degrees
	Bit5 = 90 to 112.5 degrees
	Bit6 = 112.5 to 135 degrees
	Bit7 = 135 to 157.5 degrees
	Bit8 = 157.5 to 180 degrees
	Bit9 = 180 to 202.5 degrees
	Bit10 = 202.5 to 225 degrees
	Bit11 = 225 to 247.5 degrees
	Bit12 = 247.5 to 270 degrees
	Bit13 = 270 to 292.5 degrees
	Bit14 = 292.5 to 315 degrees
	Bit15 = 315 to 337.5 degrees
	Bit16 = 337.5 to 360 degrees

columns.name: Send on More Then Value
columns.description: Threshold for a more than conditional.

columns.name: Snapshot Distance D2
columns.description:
A distance value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based in speed and distance. Two distance and speed pairs are included in the message—D1, S1 and D2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed is < S1 then distance to next snapshot is D1
•	If speed is > S2 then distance to next snapshot is D2
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then distance to snapshot is linearly interpolated between D1 and D2
•	If S1 is zero, then the distance to snapshot is always D1
This is distance D2, which is expressed meters.

columns.name: Test No.
columns.description: Testing ID Number

columns.name: Termination Time
columns.description: A time value that provides a time-to-live type of time-out. It allows users to provide the number of seconds at which time the probe management process ceases and the default condition is applied.

columns.name: Snapshot Speed S2
columns.description:
A speed value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based on elapsed time. Two time and speed pairs are included in the message—T1, S1 and T2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed < S1 then time to next snapshot is T1 – default 20 mph (8.9 m/s) and 6 seconds
•	If speed > S2 then time to next snapshot is T2 – default 60 mph (26.8 m/s) and 20 seconds
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then time to snapshot is linearly interpolated between T1 and T2
•	If S1 is zero, then the time to snapshot is always T1
This is S2, which is the instantaneous speed in meters per second when the calculation is performed.

columns.name: Snapshot Speed S1
columns.description:
A speed value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based on elapsed time. Two time and speed pairs are included in the message—T1, S1 and T2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed < S1 then time to next snapshot is T1 – default 20 mph (8.9 m/s) and 6 seconds
•	If speed > S2 then time to next snapshot is T2 – default 60 mph (26.8 m/s) and 20 seconds
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then time to snapshot is linearly interpolated between T1 and T2
•	If S1 is zero, then the time to snapshot is always T1
This is S1, which is the instantaneous speed in meters per second when the calculation is performed.

columns.name: Snapshot Distance S2
columns.description:
A speed value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based in speed and distance. Two distance and speed pairs are included in the message—D1, S1 and D2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed is < S1 then distance to next snapshot is D1
•	If speed is > S2 then distance to next snapshot is D2
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then distance to snapshot is linearly interpolated between D1 and D2
•	If S1 is zero, then the distance to snapshot is always D1
This is speed S2, which is expressed meters per second.

columns.name: Vehicle Status Request Count Number
columns.description: Indicates the value of the current sequence count number represented on this row of the CSV data file.

columns.name: Snapshot Distance D1
columns.description:
A distance value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based in speed and distance. Two distance and speed pairs are included in the message—D1, S1 and D2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed is < S1 then distance to next snapshot is D1
•	If speed is > S2 then distance to next snapshot is D2
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then distance to snapshot is linearly interpolated between D1 and D2
•	If S1 is zero, then the distance to snapshot is always D1
This is distance D1, which is expressed meters.

columns.name: Snapshot Time T1
columns.description:
A speed value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based on elapsed time. Two time and speed pairs are included in the message—T1, S1 and T2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed < S1 then time to next snapshot is T1 – default 20 mph (8.9 m/s) and 6 seconds
•	If speed > S2 then time to next snapshot is T2 – default 60 mph (26.8 m/s) and 20 seconds
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then time to snapshot is linearly interpolated between T1 and T2
•	If S1 is zero, then the time to snapshot is always T1
This is T1, which is expressed as time in seconds.

columns.name: Snapshot Time T2
columns.description:
: A speed value to be used in a calculation that allows users to change the snapshot collection policy based in elapsed time. Two time and speed pairs are included in the message—T1, S1 and T2, S2—to be used by the OBU as follows. 
•	If speed < S1 then time to next snapshot is T1 – default 20 mph (8.9 m/s) and 6 seconds
•	If speed is > S2 then time to next snapshot is T2 – default 60 mph (26.8 m/s) and 20 seconds
•	If S1 < speed < S2 then time to snapshot is linearly interpolated between T1 and T2
•	If S1 is zero, then the time to snapshot is always T1
This is T2, which is expressed as time in seconds.

description: Contains all PDCMs generated during the AMCD field testing program. The PDCM is a control message sent from the server to OBUs to customize a request for Probe Vehicle Data (PVD) from the receiving OBU. All PDCMs are generated by the VCC Cloud server and transmitted to OBU clients through either a DSRC or cellular communications channel.

description: The main dataset is a 9 MB file of trajectory data (I294_L2_final.csv) that contains position, speed, and acceleration data for small and large automated (L2) and non-automated vehicles on a highway in a suburban environment. Supporting files include aerial reference images for twelve distinct data collection “Runs” (I294_L2_Run_X_ref_image_with_lanes.png, where X equals 5, 28, 30, 36, 38, and 42 for southbound runs and 23, 29, 31, 33, 35, and 41 for northbound runs). description: This report contains tables and charts on the financial condition of the U.S. major airlines. All data presented in this financial and traffic review are derived from data reported to the U.S. Department of Transportation on Form 41 Schedules by Large Certificated Air Carriers. The data are presented on both a carrier group and an individual carrier basis, but the primary focus is on the individual major carrier and its performance. Data are presented for the most recent quarterly period and the

name: Weekly Gasoline Product Supplied
description: This dataset offers insight on weekly fluctuation of the gasoline product supply, which is an important part of any analysis of construction trends, materials and operating costs associated with highway repair and construction, and changes in traffic volume. These data come directly from the Energy Information Administration (EIA) website. The EIA publishes the average daily amount of gasoline supplied in barrels, which HPPI converts to an average number of gallons of gasoline per week.

name: 2024 NTD Annual Data - Reporter Agency Information

columns.name: TAM Tier
columns.description: Defines whether the agency is a Tier I agency required to produce their own Transit Asset Management plan (and, in parens, on what basis) or a Tier II operator eligible to be in a group TAM Plan. N/A indicates that the requirement does not apply.

columns.name: FTA Recipient ID
columns.description: The four-digit number assigned to a transit agency for the Federal Transit Administration (FTA) electronic grant making system — TrAMS (Transportation Award Management System).

columns.name: Density
columns.description: The population density of the Primary UZA of the agency, if one exists.

columns.name: Address Line 1
columns.description: First line of the agency's physical address.

columns.name: State
columns.description: State of the agency's physical address.

columns.name: Population
columns.description: The population of the Primary UZA of the agency, if one exists.

columns.name: Sq Miles
columns.description: The square miles of the Primary UZA of the agency, if one exists.

columns.name: Tribal Area Name
columns.description: Name of the Federal American Indian Areas and off-reservation trust lands, as established in US Census data and referenced in the American Community Survey, on which Tribal Transit services operate. Reported by Federally recognized Tribes reporting to the NTD for purposes of Tribal Transit Program Funding.

columns.name: UZA Name
columns.description: Name of the primary Urbanized Area (see UZA) served by the transit agency.

columns.name: Primary UZA UACE Code
columns.description: The Census Bureau Urbanized Area code assigned for the primary urbanized area served by the transit agency (see UZA Name).

columns.name: Volunteer Drivers
columns.description: Individuals who drive vehicles in revenue service to transport passengers for the transit provider but are not employees of the transit provider and are not compensated for their labor.

columns.name: Service Area Pop
columns.description: A measure of access to transit service in terms of population served. The reporting transit agency determines the service area boundaries and population for most transit services using the definitions contained in the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 (ADA), i.e. a corridor surrounding the routes 3/4 of a mile on either side, or for rail, a series of circles of radius 3/4 mile centered on each station Transit agency reporters are required to submit service area information.

columns.name: Service Area Sq Miles
columns.description: A measure of access to transit service in terms of area coverage (square miles). The reporting transit agency determines the service area boundaries and population for most transit services using the definitions contained in the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 (ADA), i.e. a corridor surrounding the routes 3/4 of a mile on either side, or for rail, a series of circles of radius 3/4 mile centered on each station Transit agency reporters are required to submit service area information.

columns.name: Universal Entity ID
columns.description: The UEID is a number or other identifier used to identify a specific commercial, nonprofit, or Government entity in the System for Award Management (SAM). This is now reported in place of DUNS number for each unique transit agency reporting to the NTD. See the U.S. General Services Administration UEID web page for more information.

columns.name: Region
columns.description: The FTA region in which the reporter is located.

columns.name: Zip Code
columns.description: Zip Code of the agency's physical address.

columns.name: P.O. Box
columns.description: The PO Box of the agency for mailing purposes (if applicable).

columns.name: Address Line 2
columns.description: Second line of the agency's physical address (if applicable).

columns.name: Number of Counties with Service
columns.description: States report the total number of counties in the state that are currently served, in whole or in part, by Formula Grants for Rural Areas (§5311)-funded operators.

columns.name: FY End Date
columns.description: The last calendar day of an agency's fiscal year.

columns.name: Reporting Module
columns.description: A general classification that will determine which, if any, FTA formula programs will use the NTD data. For example, Tribes and Native Villages will have data included in the in the §5311j Tribal Transit Program. Reporters receiving Chapter 53 funds but not receiving or benefiting from §5307 and §5311 AND not electing to report service data are classified as Asset due to the requirement to report asset inventory data. These agencies are not presently included in formula program datasets.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: Reporter Type will be based on where the agency operates and the reporting requirements associated with the agency. Much more discussion is provided in the NTD Data Publications Guide. Agencies that receive Chapter 53 funds and own, operate, or manage capital assets in public transportation are also required to file an annual report, even if they do not receive §5307 or §5311 funds. Agencies that do not receive or benefit from FTA funding may elect to submit their data to the NTD as Voluntary Reporter but are still assigned a reporter type.

columns.name: Doing Business As
columns.description: The name under which the reporting agency is doing business.

columns.name: Division/Department
columns.description: Provided in case the agency identified is engaged in other lines of business that are not reportable as public transportation to the NTD. Identifies the name of the division(s), department(s), or other organizational units providing the public transportation services included in the NTD report.

columns.name: State Admin Funds Expended
columns.description: State Departments of Transportation report the §5311 revenues they expended as a result of administering the program. Since the §5311 program operates on a reimbursement basis, revenues expended during the report year will be expended during the same year. Report the operating revenue expended during the report year from FTA §5311 Formula Grants for Rural Areas funds.

columns.name: Zip Code Ext
columns.description: Zip Code Extension of the agency's physical address (if provided).

columns.name: Reported by Name
columns.description: The name of the agency submitting the data to FTA other than the report. Often the parent reporter.

columns.name: Subrecipient Type
columns.description: Reflects the type of Rural Formula Grant funding received by the subrecipient.

columns.name: Personal Vehicles
columns.description: Vehicles that are used by the transit provider to transport passengers in revenue service but are owned by private individuals, typically an employee of the agency or a volunteer driver

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: FTA assigns each reporter a unique five-digit NTD Identification Number. The first digit of the NTD ID corresponds to the FTA Region where the reporter is located (e.g., 9#### indicates Region IX). The code will have a four-to-five digit prefix for any entity submitting the report on behalf of the reporter. For example, State Departments of Transportation (usually indicated as #R##) submit on behalf of their subrecipients.

columns.name: Reported By NTD ID
columns.description: The NTD ID of the agency submitting the data to FTA other than the reporter. Often the parent reporter.

columns.name: Organization Type
columns.description: Classifies the type of agency reporting to NTD according to pre-established categories. See NTD policy Manual, "Organization Types" for detail on each category.

columns.name: Total VOMS
columns.description: The Vehicles Operated in Maximum Service ("peak service level") across the entire fiscal year for the given agency.

columns.name: street_address_geo_reference
columns.description: A coordinate point provided for geolocation of the agency's physical address.

columns.name: US Counties
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'US Counties' (m2nu-4dib) the point in column 'street_address_geo_reference' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: URL
columns.description: The uniform resource locator for the transit agency or tranist divison's website.

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: The agency name is the full legal name of the entity filing a report to the NTD. If reporting is required under an FTA grant program, this must reflect the legal name of the funding recipient.

columns.name: Original Due Date
columns.description: The date on which the 2024 NTD Annual Report was due from the agency to FTA (considers any granted extensions).

columns.name: City
columns.description: City of the agency's mailing address.

columns.name: Number of State Counties
columns.description: The number of Counties in given State (for State Departments of Transportation).

columns.name: United States
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'United States' (8fe2-rd7y) the point in column 'street_address_geo_reference' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: US States
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'US States' (28hd-vqqn) the point in column 'street_address_geo_reference' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

name: 2023 NTD Annual Data - Facility Inventory

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: FTA assigns each reporter a unique five-digit NTD Identification Number. The first digit of the NTD ID corresponds to the FTA Region where the reporter is located (e.g., 9#### indicates Region IX). The code will have a four-to-five digit prefix for any entity submitting the report on behalf of the reporter. For example, State Departments of Transportation (usually indicated as #R##) submit on behalf of their subrecipients.

columns.name: Percent Agency Capital Responsibility
columns.description: The percentage of capital responsibility the agency is responsible for. Transit agencies have direct capital responsibility for assets that they own, jointly own with another entity, or for assets that they are responsible for replacing, overhauling, refurbishing, or conducting major repairs on that asset, or the cost of those activities are itemized as a capital line item in the agency’s budget.

columns.name: Facility Name
columns.description: Unique description provided by the agency for each facility.

columns.name: Section of a Larger Facility
columns.description: A section of a facility that varies in age from the rest of the main facility due to significant rebuilding, addition, or retrofitting. Agencies are encouraged to report sections of the facility in multiple entries to more accurately represent its age and function in the inventory. Facilities that are adjacent to one another must be reported separately.

columns.name: Street Address
columns.description: Street address of the facility.

columns.name: Administrative/Maintenance Facility Flag
columns.description: Flag denotes whether a facility serves administrative/maintenance purposes.

columns.name: Facility Type
columns.description: Each facility must be defined as a specific type. There are three categories: Administrative, Maintenance, and Passenger/Parking. Each category has multiple selections available.

columns.name: Non-Agency Mode Served
columns.description: Modes of public transit that the reporting transit agency does not operate.

columns.name: Report Year
columns.description: The year for which the data was reported.

columns.name: Passenger/Parking Facility Flag
columns.description: Flag denotes whether a facility serves passenger/parking purposes.

columns.name: Reporting Module
columns.description: A general classification that will determine which, if any, FTA formula programs will use the NTD data. For example, Tribes and Native Villages will have data included in the in the §5311j Tribal Transit Program. Reporters receiving Chapter 53 funds but not receiving or benefiting from §5307 and §5311 AND not electing to report service data are classified as Asset due to the requirement to report asset inventory data. These agencies are not presently included in formula program datasets.

columns.name: Private Modes Served
columns.description: Non-public transit modes that serve the facility

columns.name: Primary Mode Served
columns.description: The mode in which the facility is predominantly used.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: Reporter Type will be based on where they operate and the reporting requirements associated with their agency. Much more discussion is provided in the NTD Data Publications Guide.  Agencies that receive Chapter 53 funds and own, operate, or manage capital assets in public transportation are also required to file an annual report, even if they do not receive §5307 or §5311 funds. Agencies that do not receive or benefit from FTA funding may elect to submit their data to the NTD as Voluntary Reporter but are still assigned a reporter type.

columns.name: State/Parent NTD ID
columns.description: The entity, usually a State, submitting an NTD report on behalf of another entity, usually a subrecipient of the State.

columns.name: Condition Assessment
columns.description: Transit agencies are required to report a condition assessment for all facilities for which they have capital replacement responsibility. The condition assessment is based on FTA’s Transit Economic Requirements Model or TERM scale. Blank: Agency is not reporting a condition assessment for this asset yet (applies until Report Year 2022). N/A: Agency is not required to report a condition assessment because the agency has no capital replacement responsibility.

columns.name: Number of Parking Spaces
columns.description: The number of parking spaces at the facility.

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: The agency name is the full legal name of the agency. If reporting is required under an FTA grant program, this must reflect the legal name of the funding recipient.

columns.name: Square Feet
columns.description: The square footage of the facility.

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Additional information about the facility or description if "Other" or a combined facility type is selected.

columns.name: State
columns.description: State where the facility is located.

columns.name: ZIP Code
columns.description: Zip Code where the facility is located.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude of the physical location of the facility.

columns.name: Condition Assessment Date
columns.description: The date the condition assessment was completed. If not recorded, can be estimated.

columns.name: City
columns.description: City where the facility is located.

columns.name: Facility ID
columns.description: Unique ID number assigned to each facility. This number is only generated once the facility is created and saved.

columns.name: Secondary Modes Served
columns.description: Other modes in which the facility is used, however to a lesser degree than the primary mode.

columns.name: Year Built or Reconstructed as New
columns.description: The year in which facility was built or the year the transit agency reinvested in the facility to enhance its reliability or extend its useful life.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Longitude of the physical location of the facility.

description: The Facility Inventory dataset details all facilities supporting public transit service as reported to the National Transit Database (NTD) by each public transit agency in the 2023 report year. This file is also published at https://www.transit.dot.gov/ntd/ntd-data, under the Product Type of "Annual Database (Excel)." Equivalent datasets from 2018 through 2022 can also be found using that link. If you have any other questions about this table, please contact the NTD Help Desk at NTDHelp@dot.gov.

Urbanized areas do not conform to congressional districts or any other political boundaries.

columns.name: Field
columns.description: The type of data represented in the Value column. Fares represents the amount of funds earned, expended on operations, or expended on capital that was funded by passenger fares. Operating Expenses represents all expenses associated with vehicle operations, revenue and non-revenue service, vehicle maintenance, and facility maintenance. Passenger Miles represents the cumulative sum of the distances ridden by each passenger. Unlinked Passenger Trips represents the number of passengers who board public transportation vehicles. Passengers are counted each time they board a vehicle no matter how many vehicles they use to travel from their origin to their destination. Vehicle Revenue Hours represents the hours that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service plus deadhead hours. VRH includes revenue service, deadhead, and layover/recovery time. VRH excludes hours for charter service, school bus service, operator training, and vehicle maintenance testing. Vehicle Revenue Miles represents the miles that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service, including revenue service and excluding deadhead, operator training, maintenance testing, and school bus and charter services. Vehicles Operating in Maximum Service represents the maximum number of trains actually operated to provide service during the year.

description: This dataset details service data and operating expenses, broken out by agency and mode, reported to the National Transit Database for the 2024 report year. name: 2024 NTD Annual Data - Service Data and Operating Expenses by Agency (Time Series 2.2)

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: The transit agency's name.

columns.name: Last Active Report Year
columns.description: The most recent report year the reporter filed an annual report for.

columns.name: Primary UZA Name
columns.description: The name of the urbanized area the area the agency primarily serves.

columns.name: UACE Code
columns.description: An area defined by the U.S. Census Bureau that includes:  • One or more incorporated cities, villages, and towns (central place) • The adjacent densely settled surrounding territory (urban fringe) that together has a minimum of 50,000 persons  The urban fringe generally consists of contiguous territory having a density of at least 1,000 persons per square mile. Urbanized areas do not conform to congressional districts or any other political boundaries.

columns.name: UZA Population
columns.description: The population of the urbanized area the agency primarily serves.

columns.name: Field
columns.description: The type of data represented in the Value column. Fares represents the amount of funds earned, expended on operations, or expended on capital that was funded by passenger fares. Operating Expenses represents all expenses associated with vehicle operations, revenue and non-revenue service, vehicle maintenance, and facility maintenance. Passenger Miles represents the cumulative sum of the distances ridden by each passenger. Unlinked Passenger Trips represents the number of passengers who board public transportation vehicles. Passengers are counted each time they board a vehicle no matter how many vehicles they use to travel from their origin to their destination. Vehicle Revenue Hours represents the hours that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service plus deadhead hours. VRH includes revenue service, deadhead, and layover/recovery time. VRH excludes hours for charter service, school bus service, operator training, and vehicle maintenance testing. Vehicle Revenue Miles represents the miles that vehicles/passenger cars travel while in revenue service, including revenue service and excluding deadhead, operator training, maintenance testing, and school bus and charter services. Vehicles Operating in Maximum Service represents the maximum number of trains actually operated to provide service during the year.

columns.name: Reporting Module
columns.description: A general classification that determines which, if any, FTA formula programs will use the NTD data.

columns.name: Census Year
columns.description: The most recent census year from which the UZA and population data is derived	.

columns.name: UZA Area SQ Miles
columns.description: The square mileage of the urbanized area the agency primarily serves.

columns.name: Agency Status
columns.description: Designates agencies as active or inactive based on whether or not they submitted a report in the most recent report year.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: The five-digit number that is unique to each transit system reporting to the NTD. The first digit represents the FTA Region number, with "0" for Region 10.  The remaining four digits are assigned sequentially. Note the four digit prefix before the NTD ID for Rural Reporters is the reporting ID of the State DOT in which the rural public transit operator is based.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The state in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: City
columns.description: The city in which the agency is headquartered.

columns.name: Value
columns.description: The amount of the data represented in the Field column for the given Report Year.

columns.name: Report Year
columns.description: The year in which the data was reported.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: The type of NTD report that the agency completed this year.

description: This dataset details service data and operating expenses, broken out by agency, reported to the National Transit Database for the 2024 report year. NTD Data Tables organize and summarize data from the 2024 National Transit Database in a manner that is more useful for quick reference and summary analysis. If you have any other questions about this table, please contact the NTD Help Desk at NTDHelp@dot.gov.

name: 2024 NTD Annual Data - Facility Inventory

columns.name: Private Modes Served
columns.description: Non-public transit modes that serve the facility

columns.name: City
columns.description: City where the facility is located.

columns.name: Percent Agency Capital Responsibility
columns.description: The percentage of capital responsibility the agency is responsible for. Transit agencies have direct capital responsibility for assets that they own, jointly own with another entity, or for assets that they are responsible for replacing, overhauling, refurbishing, or conducting major repairs on that asset, or the cost of those activities are itemized as a capital line item in the agency’s budget.

columns.name: NTD ID
columns.description: FTA assigns each reporter a unique five-digit NTD Identification Number. The first digit of the NTD ID corresponds to the FTA Region where the reporter is located (e.g., 9#### indicates Region IX). The code will have a four-to-five digit prefix for any entity submitting the report on behalf of the reporter. For example, State Departments of Transportation (usually indicated as #R##) submit on behalf of their subrecipients.

columns.name: Condition Assessment Date
columns.description: The date the condition assessment was completed. If not recorded, can be estimated.

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: The agency name is the full legal name of the agency. If reporting is required under an FTA grant program, this must reflect the legal name of the funding recipient.

columns.name: Passenger/Parking Facility Flag
columns.description: Flag denotes whether a facility serves passenger/parking purposes.

columns.name: Condition Assessment
columns.description: Transit agencies are required to report a condition assessment for all facilities for which they have capital replacement responsibility. The condition assessment is based on FTA’s Transit Economic Requirements Model or TERM scale. Blank: Agency is not reporting a condition assessment for this asset yet (applies until Report Year 2022). N/A: Agency is not required to report a condition assessment because the agency has no capital replacement responsibility.

columns.name: State/Parent NTD ID
columns.description: The entity, usually a State, submitting an NTD report on behalf of another entity, usually a subrecipient of the State.

columns.name: Report Year
columns.description: The year for which the data was reported.

columns.name: Facility ID
columns.description: Unique ID number assigned to each facility. This number is only generated once the facility is created and saved.

columns.name: Non-Agency Mode Served
columns.description: Modes of public transit that the reporting transit agency does not operate.

columns.name: Administrative/Maintenance Facility Flag
columns.description: Flag denotes whether a facility serves administrative/maintenance purposes.

columns.name: Reporting Module
columns.description: A general classification that will determine which, if any, FTA formula programs will use the NTD data. For example, Tribes and Native Villages will have data included in the in the §5311j Tribal Transit Program. Reporters receiving Chapter 53 funds but not receiving or benefiting from §5307 and §5311 AND not electing to report service data are classified as Asset due to the requirement to report asset inventory data. These agencies are not presently included in formula program datasets.

columns.name: Reporter Type
columns.description: Reporter Type will be based on where they operate and the reporting requirements associated with their agency. Much more discussion is provided in the NTD Data Publications Guide.  Agencies that receive Chapter 53 funds and own, operate, or manage capital assets in public transportation are also required to file an annual report, even if they do not receive §5307 or §5311 funds. Agencies that do not receive or benefit from FTA funding may elect to submit their data to the NTD as Voluntary Reporter but are still assigned a reporter type.

columns.name: Primary Mode Served
columns.description: The mode in which the facility is predominantly used.

columns.name: Secondary Modes Served
columns.description: Other modes in which the facility is used, however to a lesser degree than the primary mode.

columns.name: Facility Type
columns.description: Each facility must be defined as a specific type. There are three categories: Administrative, Maintenance, and Passenger/Parking. Each category has multiple selections available.

columns.name: Facility Name
columns.description: Unique description provided by the agency for each facility.

columns.name: Street Address
columns.description: Street address of the facility.

columns.name: State
columns.description: State where the facility is located.

columns.name: ZIP Code
columns.description: Zip Code where the facility is located.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Latitude of the physical location of the facility.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Longitude of the physical location of the facility.

columns.name: Square Feet
columns.description: The square footage of the facility.

columns.name: Number of Parking Spaces
columns.description: The number of parking spaces at the facility.

columns.name: Section of a Larger Facility
columns.description: A section of a facility that varies in age from the rest of the main facility due to significant rebuilding, addition, or retrofitting. Agencies are encouraged to report sections of the facility in multiple entries to more accurately represent its age and function in the inventory. Facilities that are adjacent to one another must be reported separately.

columns.name: Year Built or Reconstructed as New
columns.description: The year in which facility was built or the year the transit agency reinvested in the facility to enhance its reliability or extend its useful life.

columns.name: Notes
columns.description: Additional information about the facility or description if "Other" or a combined facility type is selected.

description: The Facility Inventory dataset details all facilities supporting public transit service as reported to the National Transit Database (NTD) by each public transit agency in the 2024 report year. This file is also published at https://www.transit.dot.gov/ntd/ntd-data, under the Product Type of "Annual Database (Excel)." Equivalent datasets from 2018 through 2022 can also be found using that link. If you have any other questions about this table, please contact the NTD Help Desk at NTDHelp@dot.gov.

columns.name: features_properties__v_id
columns.description: In OpenSideWalks (OSW) format , edges are linear features that also contain metadata to identify them as network (graph) edges. This means that they must have two node-referencing fields: _u_id and _v_id, which mean "this linear feature begins at the Node with _id of _u_id and ends at the Node with _id of _v_id. Therefore, a network (graph) may be constructed from a set of Nodes and Edges directly from metadata. Outside of the graph representation, edges must have a unique (within the dataset) _id field.

columns.name: features_properties__u_id
columns.description: In OpenSideWalks (OSW) format , edges are linear features that also contain metadata to identify them as network (graph) edges_ This means that they must have two node-referencing fields: _u_id and _v_id, which mean "this linear feature begins at the Node with _id of _u_id and ends at the Node with _id of _v_id. Therefore, a network (graph) may be constructed from a set of Nodes and Edges directly from metadata. Outside of the graph representation, edges must have a unique (within the dataset) _id field.

description: This dataset is a list of Department of Transportation (DOT) Consolidated Artificial Intelligence (AI) use cases. The Advancing American AI Act, Executive Order (EO) 13960, Promoting the Use of Trustworthy Artificial Intelligence in the Federal Government, and Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Memorandum M-25-21, Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust, require Federal agencies to report on their use of artificial intelligence (AI).

name: Texas 4.2 Work Zone Data Feed Example
description: This dataset provides archived lane closure occurrences within the Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT) highway system in a tabular format. The ITS JPO has collections from 23 states including Texas covering various parts of the time period from 10/2019 to 08/2024 depending on when the feed was active. The data is split into two archive files, the raw data contains the collection of .json or .geojson files exactly as they were on the individual state’s WZDx feed at the time of collection.
